alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same conclusion about early maturity and the need for further development and evaluation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a need for more interdisciplinary research teams and evaluation of artificial intelligence projects in 'real world' practice settings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Research on AI for primary care is at an early stage of maturity and requires more interdisciplinary research teams and evaluation studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same recommendation but expresses it with somewhat lower stated certainty (high -> medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Acute kidney injury (AKI) occurred in 46% of hospitalized COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AKI occurred in 1835 (46%) of 3993 hospitalized patients with COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides the same percentage (with counts), so the result is unchanged aside from added raw counts.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In-hospital mortality in patients with AKI was 41% overall and 52% in intensive care. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In-hospital mortality was 50% among patients with AKI versus 8% among those without AKI. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports different mortality percentages (50% for AKI) than the preprint's 41%/52%, indicating the numerical result was revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adjusted odds ratio for mortality associated with AKI was 9.6 overall and 20.9 in patients receiving intensive care. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The adjusted odds ratio estimates from the preprint are not present in the publication claims list and appear to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 56% of patients with AKI who were discharged alive recovered kidney function back to baseline. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Only 30% of patients with AKI survived with recovery of kidney function by the time of discharge. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The recovery proportion changed substantially (56% -> 30%) and the publication reports the result with higher certainty, so the claim was revised and confidence amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine-learned model using admission features had an area under the curve of 0.79 for predicting dialysis requirement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claim list does not include the reported predictive-model AUC, indicating this preprint result was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In ME/CFS cases, there is evidence of peroxisomal dysfunction with decreased levels of plasmalogens. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Subjects in the ME/CFS group had significantly decreased levels of plasmalogens, phospholipid ethers, phosphatidylcholines, and sphingomyelins, and elevated levels of dicarboxylic acids. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms decreased plasmalogens and lists additional lipid changes, broadening the specific lipid result while preserving the original finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Decreased levels of several membrane lipids, including phosphatidylcholines and sphingomyelins, may indicate dysregulation of the cytidine-5’-diphosphocholine pathway. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our findings provide the first metabolomic evidence of peroxisomal dysfunction and are consistent with dysregulation of lipid remodeling and the tricarboxylic acid cycle. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication interprets the lipid changes in the context of broader lipid remodeling and TCA-cycle dysregulation rather than the specific CDP-choline pathway proposed in the preprint, and states the conclusion with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using machine learning algorithms with selected metabolites as predictors, we were able to differentiate female ME/CFS cases from female controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using machine learning algorithms, we were able to differentiate ME/CFS or subgroups of ME/CFS from controls with area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) values up to 0.873. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same ML discrimination claim and adds performance metrics (AUC), thereby clarifying and quantifying the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings highlight new abnormalities that may provide insights into the pathogenesis of ME/CFS. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings, if validated in other cohorts, could provide new insights into the pathogenesis of ME/CFS and highlight the potential use of the plasma metabolome as a source of biomarkers for the disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a validation caveat and explicitly mentions biomarker potential, clarifying and slightly extending the preprint's conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fully trained MLP model achieved accurate predictions for AKI (77.9%), bleeding (86.5%), death (90.3%), and any adverse outcome (80.6%) in the independent test set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fully trained MLP model achieved accurate predictions for AKI (77.9%), bleeding (86.5%), death (90.3%), and any adverse outcome (80.6%) in the independent test set.
alignments.rationale: Texts are essentially identical with the same performance numbers and claim framing, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction of stroke was not satisfactory, achieving only 69.9% accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Prediction of stroke was not satisfactory, achieving only 69.9% accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but publication lowers stated certainty from high to medium, so the claim content is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our models showed a significantly higher AUC, IDI, and NRI compared to currently used models for AKI, bleeding, and death prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our models showed a significantly higher AUC, IDI, and Brier score compared to currently used models for AKI, bleeding, and death prediction.
alignments.rationale: The comparison claim remains but one reported metric (NRI) is replaced by Brier score in the publication, indicating a substantive revision of the metrics reported while keeping scope and confidence similar.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural network-based models can accurately predict major adverse events after PCI.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using neural network-based models, we accurately predict major adverse events after PCI.
alignments.rationale: Wording is slightly rephrased but the core claim and strength remain the same, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Larger studies for replicability and longitudinal studies for evidence of impact are needed to establish these artificial intelligence methods in current PCI practice.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Larger studies for replicability and longitudinal studies for evidence of impact are needed to establish these artificial intelligence methods in current PCI practice.
alignments.rationale: The limitation statement is identical between preprint and publication, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Image resolution and contrast-to-noise ratio between brain and CSF predict the likelihood of an image being characterized as useful for hydrocephalus treatment planning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Image resolution and contrast-to-noise ratio between brain and CSF predict the likelihood of an image being characterized as useful for hydrocephalus treatment planning.
alignments.rationale: Claim text and meaning are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning enhancement substantially increases contrast-to-noise ratio improving the apparent likelihood of the image being useful.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning enhancement substantially increases contrast-to-noise ratio improving the apparent likelihood of the image being useful.
alignments.rationale: Direct match in content and strength between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning enhancement introduces structural errors which create a substantial risk of misleading clinical interpretation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning enhancement introduces structural errors which create a substantial risk of misleading clinical interpretation.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim about structural errors and clinical risk in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Images with lower quality than is customarily acceptable can be useful for hydrocephalus treatment planning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Images with lower quality than is customarily acceptable can be useful for hydrocephalus treatment planning.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about lower-quality images being useful is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Low quality images may be preferable to images enhanced with deep learning, since they do not introduce the risk of misleading information which could misguide treatment decisions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Low quality images may be preferable to images enhanced with deep learning, since they do not introduce the risk of misleading information which could misguide treatment decisions.
alignments.rationale: The comparative recommendation and its framing are the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network model successfully predicted with 69.9% accuracy the occurrence of a fall-related injury event during the selection course from the selected descriptors of the posture.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network model successfully predicted with 69.9% accuracy the occurrence of a fall-related injury event during the selection course from the selected descriptors of the posture.
alignments.rationale: Performance claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The area under the curve (AUC) value was 0.731, indicating a moderate level of predictive performance for the model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The area under the curve value was 0.731, indicating a moderate level of predictive performance of the model.
alignments.rationale: Same numeric AUC and interpretation across versions, so unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: If confirmed with a larger sample, these findings suggest that probing the posture using static posturography and machine learning-based analysis might contribute to inform risk assessment of fall-related injury during military training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Probing the posture using static posturography and machine learning-based analysis might contribute to informing risk assessment of fall-related injury during military training.
alignments.rationale: The publication shortens and slightly strengthens the phrasing (higher stated certainty), moving from conditional low-certainty wording to a clearer contribution statement, so it is clarified with amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This proof of concept study shows that analyzing the posture with machine learning can predict the risk of fall-related injury at the individual level.
alignments.rationale: No matching claim about individual-level proof-of-concept prediction appears in the publication; related conclusions were consolidated or omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings may prompt the development of novel programs for personalized injury prevention in military settings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings could ultimately lead to the development of novel programmes for personalised injury prevention in the military population.
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases the potential impact more cautiously (lower certainty) and slightly reframes audience, so the claim is clarified and the confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: iEEG-recon accurately reconstructs electrodes in both electrocorticography (ECoG) and stereoelectroencephalography (SEEG) cases with a 10 minute running time per case.
alignments.pub_claim_text: iEEG-recon accurately reconstructs electrodes in both electrocorticography and stereoelectroencephalography cases with a 30-min running time per case.
alignments.rationale: The functionality claim is the same but the reported runtime changes from 10 to 30 minutes, a substantive factual revision to the performance characteristic.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: iEEG-recon generates quality assurance reports and visualizations to support epilepsy surgery discussions.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state that the tool generates QA reports and visualizations as a standalone claim, so this specific claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reconstruction outputs from the clinical module of iEEG-recon were radiologically validated through pre- and post-implant T1-MRI visual inspections.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reconstruction outputs from the clinical module of iEEG-recon were radiologically validated through pre- and postimplant T1-MRI visual inspections.
alignments.rationale: The radiological validation claim is present with equivalent wording and emphasis in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: iEEG-recon promotes efficient data analysis and integration into clinical workflows.
alignments.pub_claim_text: iEEG-recon's accuracy, speed, and compatibility with cloud platforms make it a useful resource for epilepsy centers worldwide.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands and strengthens the workflow claim to emphasize global usefulness, speed and cloud compatibility, broadening scope and amplifying confidence compared to the more modest preprint wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI model detected pneumothorax with an AUC of 0.979, achieving 94.3% sensitivity and 92.0% specificity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI model detected any pneumothorax with an AUC of 0.979, sensitivity of 94.3%, and specificity of 92.0%.
alignments.rationale: Performance metrics and detection claim are the same; minor wording difference ('any pneumothorax') does not change the claim meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI model detected tension pneumothorax with an AUC of 0.987, achieving 94.5% sensitivity and 95.3% specificity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI model detected tension pneumothorax with an AUC of 0.987, sensitivity of 94.5%, and specificity of 95.3%.
alignments.rationale: Identical performance claim for tension pneumothorax across versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The assessed AI model accurately detected pneumothorax and tension pneumothorax on the CXR dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The assessed AI model accurately detected pneumothorax and tension pneumothorax in the chest radiograph data set.
alignments.rationale: The overall detection conclusion is the same, but the publication lowers the certainty level from high to medium, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of the AI model in clinical workflow could lead to earlier identification and improved care for patients with pneumothorax.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model's use in the clinical workflow could lead to earlier identification and improved care for patients with pneumothorax.
alignments.rationale: The practical implication claim is preserved but the publication expresses it with lower certainty (medium to low), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 63% of comments submitted on the FDA proposed framework came from parties with financial ties to industry. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 63% of comments submitted on the FDA proposed framework came from parties with financial ties to industry. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication text are identical in content and emphasis, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No financial ties were disclosed in any comments that were not from industry submitters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: No financial ties were disclosed in any of the comments that were not from industry submitters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same factual statement with equivalent wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 86% of submitted comments did not cite any scientific literature. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 86% of submitted comments did not cite any scientific literature. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric result and scope are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The FDA should mandate disclosure of potential conflicts of interest in comments to ensure regulatory frameworks best serve patient interests. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The FDA should mandate disclosure of potential conflicts of interest in comments to better serve patient interests. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the policy recommendation with slightly reworded phrasing but the intended conclusion and confidence remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Only 4% of comments cited a systematic review or meta-analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Scientific evidence, including systematic reviews and meta-analyses, were rarely cited in the comments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same finding in broader language (rarely cited) rather than the exact percentage, representing a wording clarification rather than a substantive disagreement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning-based method achieved an AUC of 0.73 for predicting non-lung-cancer mortality, outperforming human predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning-based method achieved an AUC of 0.73 for predicting non-lung-cancer mortality, outperforming human predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result and comparative claim are reproduced verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models utilized both 3D CT scans and clinical information to improve mortality prediction accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the specific claim about combining 3D CT scans with clinical information, so this preprint claim appears omitted in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 3D saliency maps can assist clinicians and radiologists in identifying regions of concern on CT scans that may indicate the need for preventative healthcare management strategies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can highlight regions in the thorax that contribute to mortality, which may be overlooked by clinicians. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the model's ability to highlight thoracic regions linked to mortality and clinician oversight, which clarifies and narrows the preprint's broader statement about assisting preventative management.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepSurv significantly improved ASCVD risk prediction for major adverse events (AUC: 0.82 vs. 0.79, P ≤0.001) and mortality (AUC: 0.86 vs. 0.80, P ≤0.001) compared to the COXPH model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepSurv significantly improved ASCVD risk prediction for major adverse events (AUC: 0.82 vs. 0.80, P ≤ 0.001) and mortality (AUC: 0.87 vs. 0.84, P ≤ 0.001) compared to the COXPH model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same directional improvement but provides different AUC numbers, so the claim is a revised numeric result rather than identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Implementing non-categorical NRI, there was a 65% increase in correct reclassification compared to the COXPH model for both major adverse events and mortality (P ≤0.05). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Implementing non-categorical NRI, there was a greater than 40% increase in correct reclassification compared to the COXPH model for both major adverse events and mortality (P ≤ 0.05). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The direction of improved reclassification is preserved, but the magnitude reported is reduced in the publication, so the numeric claim is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepSurv was the only learning algorithm to develop a significantly improved risk score criteria, outperforming COXPH for both major adverse events (4.07 vs. 2.66, P ≤0.001) and mortality (6.28 vs. 4.67, P =0.014). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepSurv developed a significantly improved risk score criteria that outcompeted COXPH for both major adverse events (4.22 vs. 3.61, P = 0.043) and mortality (6.81 vs. 5.52, P = 0.044). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the conclusion that DeepSurv outperformed COXPH but reports different metric values and p-values, indicating a revision of the reported results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The addition of inflammatory or imaging biomarkers to traditional risk factors showed minimal/no significant improvement in model prediction. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The addition of inflammatory or imaging biomarkers to traditional risk factors showed minimal or no significant improvement in model prediction. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The finding about biomarkers is stated the same way in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepSurv can leverage simple office-based clinical features alone to accurately predict ASCVD risk and cardiovascular outcomes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepSurv can accurately predict ASCVD risk and cardiovascular outcomes using simple office-based clinical features alone. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is equivalent in wording and implication between the preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PheVis is an automated unsupervised algorithm that identifies patient medical conditions from EHR data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PheVis combines diagnosis codes with medical concepts extracted from medical notes to provide an interpretable predictor of the occurrence probability for medical conditions at each visit. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands on the original description by specifying inputs and interpretability, clarifying how PheVis operates while preserving the core contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PheVis achieves cross-validated AUROC scores of 0.947 for rheumatoid arthritis and 0.987 for tuberculosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PheVis achieves cross-validated AUROC scores of 0.943 for rheumatoid arthritis and 0.987 for tuberculosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The results are the same in direction and significance but one numeric value (RA AUROC) is slightly different in the publication, so the claim is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PheVis performs significantly better than state-of-the-art methods, particularly for chronic diseases. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PheVis achieves significantly better performance than state-of-the-art unsupervised methods, especially for chronic diseases. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies 'unsupervised methods' and reiterates the chronic-disease advantage, clarifying the comparator while preserving the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The absence of exclusion of past medical history by natural language processing tools limits PheVis's performance for acute conditions in French. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PheVis performs well for chronic conditions but its performance for acute conditions is limited due to the absence of exclusion of past medical history. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation is described equivalently in both documents, with the publication restating the same constraint and language.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Small average changes in mood from pre-to mid-pandemic obfuscate substantial consequences for specific sub-populations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are relatively small population-level differences in mood assessment scores pre- to peak-UK lockdown, but larger differences exist for specific groups such as older adults and people with lower incomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey the same finding: small average changes mask larger subgroup differences; the publication provides specific subgroup examples without changing the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The coping strategies that people find helpful during the pandemic are diverse yet predictable. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report a claim about the diversity or predictability of coping strategies, so this preprint claim appears omitted from the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining psychological and demographic variables can identify individuals at most risk of adverse consequences. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mental health can be statistically predicted from factors such as age, demographics, home and work circumstances, pre-existing conditions, maladaptive technology use, and personality traits. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication lists specific predictor categories and frames the result as predictable mental health outcomes; this clarifies and specifies the preprint's conclusion about combined variable prediction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individually tailored advice can be extracted from the collective lived experiences of the general population. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A holistic view incorporating a broad range of relevant population factors can better characterise individuals whose mental health is most at risk during the COVID-19 pandemic. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the conclusion more conservatively as improved characterisation via holistic data rather than explicitly stating extraction of tailored advice; this represents a clarification of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A multi-task deep neural network was trained and validated to automate the quality control process of a large retrospective cohort of prostate cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A multi-task deep neural network was trained and validated to automate the quality control process of a large retrospective cohort of prostate cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text of P0 and Q0 is effectively identical describing the same model training and validation for automated QC.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quality assessment pipeline achieved a Pearson correlation of 0.89 for overall usability with specialist urological pathologists. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The quality assessment model, PathProfiler, achieved a Pearson correlation of 0.89 for overall usability with specialist pathologists. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same numeric result and evaluation against specialist pathologists; wording difference is minor (urological vs pathologists).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model, designated as PathProfiler, indicates comparable predicted usability of images from the assessed cohorts, ranging from 86-90%. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PathProfiler predicts that 86–90% of WSIs from the assessed cohorts are usable. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same predicted usability range and same model name; claim content matches directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PathProfiler is able to predict WSIs that could benefit from re-scanning or re-staining for quality improvement. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model is capable of identifying WSIs that could benefit from interventions such as re-scanning or re-staining for quality improvement. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both claims state the model can flag slides needing re-scan/re-stain; publication wording adds 'interventions' but meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrates that AI can automate the quality control process of large retrospective cohorts to maximize research outputs and conclusions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication does not restate this broader conclusion about maximizing research outputs; the specific automation results are present but the broader claim is not explicitly made.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed multi-task deep learning framework using sMRI and cognitive assessment achieved 74.9±4.3% five-fold cross-validated accuracy in classifying early psychosis patients from healthy individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep learning model achieved 74.9 ± 4.3% five-fold cross-validated accuracy in classifying early psychosis using structural MRI and cognitive assessments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric result, modalities, and evaluation protocol are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inclusion of cognitive estimations in the classification model improved the area under the curve to 71.1±4.1% for early psychosis classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The area under the curve for early psychosis classification was 71.1 ± 4.1% when cognitive estimations were included. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same numeric AUC result and same attribution to inclusion of cognitive estimations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study reveals the feasibility of automated cognitive estimation using sMRI through deep learning models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study reveals the feasibility of automated cognitive estimation using structural MRI through deep learning models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical conclusion about feasibility of automated cognitive estimation from sMRI.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model does not require time-consuming volumetric or surface-based analysis, simplifying the classification process. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication does not explicitly assert the absence of volumetric/surface-based analysis; that simplification claim is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two studies reported improved mortality prediction using Glasgow Coma Scale and biomarkers such as Neutrophil to Lymphocyte Ratio and glucose. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two studies reported improved mortality prediction using Glasgow Coma Scale and biomarkers such as Neutrophil to Lymphocyte Ratio and glucose. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claims are identical in content and specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: One study reported that metabolic biomarkers Ornithine, Symmetric Dimethylarginine, and Dimethylguanidine Valeric acid were associated with poor outcomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: One study reported that metabolic biomarkers Ornithine, Symmetric Dimethylarginine, and Dimethylguanidine Valeric acid were associated with poor outcomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match; identical reported biomarkers and association.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nine studies reported improved prediction of complications and reduction in latency until intervention using clinical scores and imaging. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nine studies reported improved prediction of complications and reduction in latency until intervention using clinical scores and imaging. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claims are the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Four studies reported accurate prediction of aneurysmal rupture based on size, shape, and CNN. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four studies reported accurate prediction of aneurysmal rupture based on size, shape, and CNN. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical reporting of the four studies and their predictors.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI/ML technologies possess tremendous potential in accelerating SAH systems-of-care. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI/ML technologies possess tremendous potential in accelerating SAH systems-of-care. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same concluding statement about AI/ML potential.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrated high predictive accuracy of synchrony in facial movements for distinguishing between autistic and non-autistic individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The two prediction models based on reciprocal adaptation in facial movements and individual amounts of head and body motion and facial expressiveness showed the highest precision with balanced accuracies of 79.5% and 68.8%, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication provides model-specific balanced accuracies and model descriptions that clarify and narrow the original general claim of 'high predictive accuracy.'

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings underline the distinctive nature of non-verbal behavior in autism. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our work highlights the distinctive nature of non-verbal behavior in autism and its utility for digital phenotyping-based classification. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the distinctiveness claim and further emphasizes utility for digital phenotyping, thereby clarifying and broadening the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of computer vision algorithms in analyzing social interactions provides a feasible approach for digitalized assessment of autism. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our work highlights the distinctive nature of non-verbal behavior in autism and its utility for digital phenotyping-based classification. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication elevates feasibility to demonstrated utility for digital phenotyping; the claim is therefore clarified and confidence appears amplified by emphasizing practical utility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Computerized Tomography (CT) has a prognostic role in the early diagnosis of COVID-19 due to its fast and accurate results. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Computed tomography has a prognostic role in the early diagnosis of COVID-19 by providing rapid and accurate results. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same conclusion about CT providing rapid and accurate results for early COVID-19 diagnosis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study achieves the best performance with 99% accuracy for lung segmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication does not report the specific lung segmentation accuracy claim; this specific metric is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The improvements in the study lead to the highest rates in terms of accuracy (99.8%), precision (99.8%), recall (99.8%), f1-score (99.8%), and roc acu (99.9979%) using deep learning methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of COVID-19 patient detection from chest CT images using various deep learning systems is extremely optimistic. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Preprint reports very specific near-perfect metrics; publication does not present those numbers and instead offers a more cautious/general statement about optimistic accuracies, indicating a tempered revision.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DenseNet169 achieves the best performance among deep convolutional neural networks with 99.8% accuracy for automatic COVID-19 classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of COVID-19 patient detection from chest CT images using various deep learning systems is extremely optimistic. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Specific model-level top-performance claim in the preprint is not supported in the publication which instead makes a general, more cautious statement about model accuracies.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: InceptionResNetV2 and Xception achieve the second and third-best performance with accuracies of 99.65% and 99.60%, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of COVID-19 patient detection from chest CT images using various deep learning systems is extremely optimistic. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication does not report the detailed ranking and near-perfect accuracies for these architectures; it instead gives a generalized, more cautious appraisal of reported accuracies.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A BERT-based classifier achieved the highest accuracies of 83.9% for Corpus-1 and 86.4% for Corpus-2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A classifier based on BERT obtained the highest accuracies of 81.7% for Corpus 1 and 80.7% for Corpus 2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims report the same BERT result but with different accuracy values in the publication (lower), so the core claim is revised (numeric change) and confidence is tempered by lower reported performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed automatic pipeline for categorizing Medicaid-related tweets can be generalized for health service programs other than Medicaid. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed system for automatic categorization can be deployed and generalized for health service programs other than Medicaid. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the same generalization claim and adds the notion of deployment, clarifying applicability without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Post-classification analyses indicated differing inter-corpora distributions of tweet categories, with political and consumer-feedback tweets being the most frequent. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Postclassification analyses revealed differing intercorpora distributions of tweet categories, with political and consumer feedback tweets being the most frequent. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same finding but assigns higher certainty; content is unchanged while reported confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The broad and variable content of Medicaid-related tweets necessitates automatic categorization to identify topic-relevant posts. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The broad and variable content of Medicaid-related tweets necessitates automatic categorization to identify topic-relevant posts. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical wording and intent in preprint and publication; no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm achieved a ROC AUC of 0.959 and a PR AUC of 0.976. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm achieved a ROC AUC of 0.959 and a PR AUC of 0.976. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact same performance metrics reported in the publication; claim content and confidence unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm demonstrated an accuracy of 0.901, a positive predictive value of 0.840, and a negative predictive value of 0.982 when classifying COVID-positive and COVID-negative cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm obtained an accuracy of 0.901, a positive predictive value of 0.840, and a very high negative predictive value of 0.982 when classifying COVID-positive and COVID-negative cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance numbers are the same; publication only adds an adjective emphasizing the high NPV, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The weakly-supervised deep learning model can accurately predict the COVID-19 infectious probability in chest CT volumes without the need for annotating the lesions for training. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed weakly-supervised deep learning model can accurately predict the COVID-19 infectious probability and discover lesion regions in chest CT. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both assert accurate prediction by a weakly-supervised model; the publication emphasizes lesion discovery but does not explicitly restate the 'without lesion annotation' wording, so the claim is reframed/clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed deep learning software provides a fast way to identify COVID-19 patients, which is beneficial to control the outbreak of SARS-CoV-2. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm processes a single patient's CT volume in only 1.93 seconds using a dedicated GPU. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Preprint makes a broad claim about outbreak control; publication reports a specific processing-time result (1.93 s). The focus is narrowed to runtime and the broader public-health conclusion is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI was correct in predicting lethal arrhythmias in 4 out of every 5 cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep neural network VA-ResNet-50 classified ECGs for ventricular arrhythmia capability with an accuracy of 0.76. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Preprint claimed ~80% correctness; publication reports accuracy 0.76 (slightly lower), so the numeric performance claim is revised and slightly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: If the AI indicated a person was at risk, their risk of a lethal event was three times higher than that of normal adults. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: If the AI indicated a person was at risk of lethal arrhythmia, the relative risk of a lethal event was 2.87 times higher than in normal adults. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a more precise relative risk estimate (2.87 vs ~3), so the claim is clarified numerically without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI performed better than current medical guidelines for risk stratification of lethal arrhythmias. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI performed better than current medical guidelines in determining the risk of lethal arrhythmia from standard heart tracings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same comparative conclusion against current guidelines is retained in the publication with similar wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep neural network VA-ResNet-50 achieved an accuracy of 0.76 in classifying electrocardiograms for ventricular arrhythmia capability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep neural network VA-ResNet-50 classified ECGs for ventricular arrhythmia capability with an accuracy of 0.76. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical accuracy claim reported in both preprint and publication; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ambulatory electrocardiograms can provide risk signals for ventricular arrhythmia risk stratification when analyzed using VA-ResNet-50. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pyramid sampling from ambulatory ECGs is hypothesized to capture autonomic activity relevant for risk stratification. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Preprint made a more definitive claim that ambulatory ECGs provide risk signals; publication frames a related mechanism as a hypothesis (pyramid sampling capturing autonomic activity), which is a more tentative formulation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study aims to critically assess the clinical accuracy, comprehensiveness, coherence, and safety of recommendations produced by leading generative AI models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study aims to critically assess recommendations produced by four leading GenAI models in the context of clinical microbiology and infectious diseases. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication specifies the number of models and narrows the context to clinical microbiology/infectious diseases, clarifying the original broader aim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Generated responses will be graded by a panel of human evaluators with expertise in clinical microbiology and infectious diseases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evaluations of AI-generated responses will be performed using a 5-point Likert scale across four clinical domains: factual consistency, comprehensiveness, coherence, and medical harmfulness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides details on the evaluation instrument and domains; while it does not restate the exact wording about the expert panel, the evaluation procedure is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study will offer insights into the feasibility, limitations, and boundaries of generative AI in healthcare. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study will provide insights into the feasibility, limitations, and boundaries of GenAI in clinical consultations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Substantively the same claim but the publication narrows the application to clinical consultations; content and confidence otherwise unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ethical guidelines and safety guardrails should be developed to uphold patient safety and clinical standards. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ethical guidelines and safety guardrails should be developed to uphold patient safety and clinical standards. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical recommendation appears in the publication with same scope and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A standardized, healthcare-specific, universal prompt template is developed to elicit clinically impactful AI responses. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The standardized prompt template is presented in the publication but was not in the preprint, so this is an added contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models can impute the PaO2/FIO2 ratio from SpO2/FIO2 with lower error and greater accuracy compared to published equations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models demonstrated greater accuracy in predicting PaO2/FiO2 ≤ 150 compared to previously published equations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication supports improved accuracy but frames it specifically for the ≤150 threshold, narrowing the original broad performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using only three features (SpO2, FiO2, and PEEP) is sufficient to accurately impute the PaO2/FIO2 ratio. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Three features (SpO2, FiO2, and PEEP) are sufficient to impute PaO2 from SpO2, compared to using seven features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication corroborates the three-feature sufficiency and explicitly contrasts it with seven-feature models; claim content and confidence remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning models demonstrated superior performance in imputing PaO2 across the entire span of SpO2 values, including those ≥ 97%. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Imputation using data from an independent validation cohort of ICU patients showed greater accuracy with machine learning models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Preprint asserted superior performance across the full SpO2 range including high saturations; the publication reports independent-cohort validation of greater accuracy but does not explicitly claim uniformly superior performance at ≥97%, so the claim is narrowed/revised and presented more cautiously.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The improved performance of machine learning algorithms suggests a promising framework for future use in large datasets. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The online calculator using machine learning algorithms can accurately impute the partial pressure of oxygen (PaO2) from the peripheral saturation of oxygen (SpO2). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents an implemented online calculator that operationalizes the claimed approach, clarifying the conclusion and providing stronger evidence (higher certainty) of practicality.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning models outperformed published equations in both Black and White patients, addressing potential hidden hypoxemia. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This subgroup performance and implication for hidden hypoxemia are reported in the publication but were not present in the preprint, so this is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models can comprehensively quantify left and right ventricular dysfunction from ECG data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning models can comprehensively quantify left and right ventricular dysfunction from ECG data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion verbatim with no change in scope or expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The area under the curve (AUC) for left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) classification in internal testing was 0.94 for LVEF<=40%, 0.82 for 40%<LVEF<=50%, and 0.89 for LVEF>50%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The area under the curve (AUC) for left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) classification in internal testing was 0.94 for LVEF 60%, 0.82 for 40% < LVEF 650%, and 0.89 for LVEF >50%. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean absolute error for LVEF regression was 5.84% in internal testing and 6.14% in external validation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean absolute error for LVEF regression was 5.84% in internal testing and 6.14% in external validation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AUC for predicting the composite right ventricular outcome was 0.84 in both internal testing and external validation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AUC for predicting the composite right ventricular outcome was 0.84 in both internal testing and external validation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The right-ventricular outcome AUC and its validation context are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning on ECG data can create inexpensive screening, diagnostic, and predictive tools for both left and right ventricular dysfunction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DL on ECG data can create inexpensive screening, diagnostic, and predictive tools for both left and right ventricular dysfunction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed algorithm based on deep convolutional neural networks achieved better sensitivity and specificity than real physicians' diagnoses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm achieved better sensitivity and specificity than diagnoses made by nine physicians under limited diseases and conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm successfully and accurately classified four subtypes of idiopathic inflammatory myopathies and hereditary muscle diseases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm successfully classified four subtypes of idiopathic inflammatory myopathies with an average AUC of 0.958 and seven subtypes of hereditary muscle disease with an average AUC of 0.936. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results suggest that the algorithm can be safely used in a clinical setting. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results support the reliability of the algorithm and suggest its potential for straightforward use in a clinical setting. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication tones down the categorical 'can be safely used' to a more cautious statement about reliability and potential for clinical use, reducing expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The similarity between the algorithm's and physicians' predictions was established using visualization technology. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method established to validate the similarity between the predictions made by the algorithm and the seven physicians clarified the validity of the predictions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's contribution as a validation method that clarifies prediction validity; the core idea (visual validation of similarity) is retained but expressed more formally.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) can predict size and intensity features of tumors with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) can predict size and intensity features of tumors with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Shape irregularity and heterogeneity features had very high prediction errors and generalized poorly when predicted by CNNs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Shape irregularity and heterogeneity features had very high prediction errors and generalized poorly when predicted by CNNs. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Learning shape features requires an order of magnitude more samples compared to intensity and size features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Learning shape features required an order of magnitude more samples compared to intensity and size features. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: CNNs may generally under-utilize the shape and texture information that is more easily captured by radiomics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: To improve CNN performance, shape and texture features can be computed explicitly and added as auxiliary variables or supplied as synthetic inputs. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: To improve CNN performance, shape and texture features can be computed explicitly and added as auxiliary variables or supplied as synthetic inputs. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoML methods demonstrate significantly better performance in the segmentation of abdominal organs compared to non-AutoML frameworks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: nnU-Net was quantitatively preferred over Auto3DSeg in a blinded evaluation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings indicate that AutoML methods have the potential to enhance the efficiency of oncological workflows. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings indicate that AutoML frameworks can enhance the efficiency of oncological workflows. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Nose-Keeper app demonstrated a remarkable overall accuracy of 92.27% in detecting nasopharyngeal carcinoma and other nasal conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The app Nose-Keeper demonstrated a remarkable overall accuracy of 92.27% in detecting nasopharyngeal carcinoma and other nasal diseases. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sensitivity and specificity of the Nose-Keeper app in NPC detection achieved 96.39% and 99.91%, respectively, outperforming nine experienced otolaryngologists. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nose-Keeper achieved a sensitivity of 96.39% and specificity of 99.91% in nasopharyngeal carcinoma detection, outperforming nine experienced otolaryngologists. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical performance and comparative claim are identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Explainable artificial intelligence was employed in the Nose-Keeper app to highlight key lesion areas, improving decision-making accuracy and safety. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Explainable artificial intelligence was employed in Nose-Keeper to highlight key lesion areas, improving decision-making accuracy and safety. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same explanation and claimed benefits of explainable AI.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nose-Keeper can assist primary healthcare providers in diagnosing nasopharyngeal carcinoma and related diseases efficiently. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nose-Keeper can assist primary healthcare providers in diagnosing nasopharyngeal carcinoma and common nasal diseases efficiently. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed unsupervised physics-informed convolutional neural network approach can connect death records with incidence. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed unsupervised physics-informed convolutional neural network approach can connect death records with incidence in epidemiological systems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach allows for the identification of regime changes at single-day resolution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach allows for the identification of regime changes at single-day resolution. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Applied to COVID-19 data, the approach can identify the implementation and removal of lockdowns and other nonpharmaceutical interventions with 0.93-day accuracy over the time span of a year. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: When applied to COVID-19 data, the approach can identify the implementation and removal of lockdowns and other nonpharmaceutical interventions with 0.93-day accuracy over the time span of a year. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Obese patients with breast cancer have a 50% to 80% increased rate of axillary nodal metastasis compared to normal weight patients. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identified morphological differences in non-metastatic axillary nodes between node-positive and node-negative obese breast cancer patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nonmetastatic lymph nodes of node-positive patients show increased average adipocyte size, increased white space between lymphocytes, and increased red blood cells. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pathology review revealed an increase in the average size of adipocytes and an increased amount of white space between lymphocytes in non-metastatic lymph nodes of node-positive breast cancer patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nonmetastatic lymph nodes of node-positive patients show increased average adipocyte size, increased white space between lymphocytes, and increased red blood cells. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Immunohistology analysis showed a decrease of CD3 expression and an increase of leptin expression in fat-replaced axillary lymph nodes in obese node-positive patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Preliminary immunohistochemistry analysis indicates a trend of decreased CD3 expression and increased leptin expression in fat-replaced axillary lymph nodes of obese, node-positive patients. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings suggest a novel direction to further investigate the interaction between lymph node adiposity, lymphatic dysfunction, and breast cancer nodal metastases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepHit can effectively handle high-dimensional data and evolving risk hazards over time with performance similar to the Cox Proportional Hazards model, achieving an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.78 for both models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepHit can effectively handle high-dimensional data and evolving risk hazards over time with performance similar to the Cox Proportional Hazards model, achieving an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.78 for both models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep survival model outperformed traditional methodology by identifying time-varied risk trajectories for preeclampsia, providing insights for early and individualized intervention. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep survival model outperformed traditional methodology by identifying time-varied risk trajectories for preeclampsia, providing insights for early and individualized intervention. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: K-means clustering resulted in patients delineating into low-risk, early-onset, and late-onset preeclampsia groups, each of which has distinct risk factors. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: K-means clustering resulted in patients delineating into low-risk, early-onset, and late-onset preeclampsia groups, each of which has distinct risk factors. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work demonstrates a novel application of deep survival analysis in time-varying prediction of preeclampsia risk. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work demonstrates a novel application of deep survival analysis in time-varying prediction of preeclampsia risk. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results highlight the advantage of deep survival models compared to Cox Proportional Hazards models in providing personalized risk trajectory. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results highlight the advantage of deep survival models compared to Cox Proportional Hazards models in providing personalized risk trajectory. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Properly retrained XGB models outperform the baseline models in all simulation scenarios, signifying the existence of data drift. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Properly retrained XGB models outperform the baseline models in all simulation scenarios, signifying the existence of data drift. (certainty=high, type=result)
