alignments.rationale: Identical forward-looking recommendation appears in both preprint and publication with the same emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There was no significant change in the AI-ECG HCM score before versus after SRT at YNHHS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There was no significant change in the AI-ECG HCM score before versus after surgical or percutaneous reduction of the interventricular septum (SRT) at multiple sites. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same null result but across multiple sites rather than a single site (YNHHS), expanding the scope of the finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI-ECG HCM scores did not improve post SRT at CCF and AHS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There was no significant change in the AI-ECG HCM score before versus after surgical or percutaneous reduction of the interventricular septum (SRT) at multiple sites. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the lack of improvement at multiple sites (including CCF and AHS), broadening the original site-specific statement while keeping the result and certainty the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The median AI-ECG score among patients on mavacamten therapy decreased significantly from 0.41 to 0.28. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The median AI-ECG score of patients on mavacamten therapy decreased significantly from 0.41 to 0.28 at the end of a median follow-up period of 237 days. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core numeric result is identical; the publication adds follow-up duration detail, clarifying the context without changing scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lack of improvement in AI-based HCM score with SRT, in contrast to a significant decrease with mavacamten, suggests the potential role of AI-ECG for serial monitoring of pathophysiological improvement in HCM. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach suggests the potential role of AI-ECG for serial point-of-care monitoring of pathophysiologic improvement after medical therapy in HCM using ECG images. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both claims posit a monitoring role for AI-ECG; the publication clarifies application as point-of-care monitoring using ECG images, adding implementation detail but keeping the underlying conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep learning system for classifying pulmonary nodules achieves an AUC of 0.938 for localization and 0.912 for differential diagnosis on the LIDC-IDRI dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system achieved an Area Under Curve (AUC) of 0.938 for PN localization and an AUC of 0.912 for PN differential diagnosis on the LIDC-IDRI dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numerical performance metrics and dataset are identical between preprint and publication; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system demonstrates comparable performance to full annotation-based diagnosis systems while significantly reducing the time and cost associated with manual annotations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed anthropomorphic diagnosis system of pulmonary nodules based on deep learning achieves comparable performance to full-annotation based diagnosis systems using weak annotation data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both state comparable performance to full-annotation systems; the publication emphasizes the use of weak annotation data, consistent with the preprint's claim about reduced annotation burden.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of handcrafted features into the deep learning networks improves the classification performance across different diameters and sub-groups of pulmonary nodules. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PN classification networks can differentiate pulmonary nodules with diverse labels, including pure ground-glass opacities, part-solid nodules, and solid nodules. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the networks' ability to differentiate nodule types but does not mention the specific contribution of handcrafted features or improvement across diameters/subgroups, indicating a revision that narrows the methodological claim to a capability statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system can efficiently localize and differentially diagnose pulmonary nodules in resource-limited environments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system demonstrates efficient localization and differential diagnosis of pulmonary nodules in a resource limited environment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about performance in resource-limited settings is restated with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The c-Triadem model demonstrates high accuracy in predicting mild cognitive impairment and Alzheimer's disease using blood-based biomarkers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The c-Triadem model demonstrates high accuracy in predicting mild cognitive impairment and Alzheimer's disease using blood-based biomarkers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in publication and preprint regarding model accuracy and input modality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's performance is superior to previous models, achieving an AUC of 97% and an accuracy of 89%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model's performance is superior to previous models, achieving an AUC of 97% and an accuracy of 89%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact performance metrics and comparative claim are retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SHAP analysis identifies CASP9, LCK, and SDC3 SNPs, along with PINK1, ATG5, and ubiquitin expression, as influential for model performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Key features identified by SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) include CASP9, LCK, and SDC3 SNPs, as well as PINK1, ATG5, and ubiquitin expression. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: List of influential features matches between preprint and publication with the same level of emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The c-Triadem model facilitates the identification of potential blood-based genetic markers related to DNA damage response and mitophagy in Alzheimer's disease. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The c-Triadem model facilitates the identification of potential blood-based genetic markers related to DNA damage response and mitophagy in Alzheimer's disease. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is preserved verbatim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can be applied for detection and biomarker identification in other related dementias. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include the broader application to other dementias present in the preprint; this forward-looking application was not carried forward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI closely mirrored human analysis, demonstrating the drug's efficacy with marginal differences (<3%) in identifying both drug efficacy and reported symptoms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI analysis closely mirrored human analysis, demonstrating the drug's efficacy with marginal differences in identifying both drug efficacy and reported symptoms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim language and content are effectively identical between preprint and publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study showcases the potential of LLMs to accurately simulate and analyze clinical trials. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study highlights the potential of AI to accurately analyze noisy clinical notes to inductively produce clinical knowledge. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's general claim about simulating/analyzing trials to a more specific claim about analyzing noisy clinical notes; certainty is reduced in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLMs can reconstruct essential trial elements, identify treatment effects, and recognize reported symptoms within a realistic clinical framework. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI successfully identified the number of seizures, symptom reports, and treatment efficacy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core identification results (symptoms, events, efficacy) but omits explicit mention of reconstructing trial elements and reports stronger certainty/quantification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings underscore the relevance of LLMs in future clinical research, offering a scalable, efficient alternative to traditional data mining methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings emphasize the relevance of AI in future clinical research, offering a scalable and efficient alternative to traditional labor-intensive data mining. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion about AI/LLMs being a scalable, efficient alternative; wording is essentially synonymous.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MyoVision-US software significantly reduces the time required for muscle ultrasound analysis from approximately 24 hours to 247 seconds, saving roughly 99.8%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The custom-built deep-learning model (MyoVision-US) significantly reduces the time required for muscle ultrasound analysis from approximately 24 hours to 247 seconds, saving 99.8% of the time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numbers and claim are the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The consistency between manual and automated analyses of muscle ultrasound images was good to excellent, with ICC values ranging from 0.85 to 0.99 for quadriceps complex and 0.93 to 0.99 for tibialis anterior measurements. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Consistency between manual and automated analyses of muscle size and quality is good to excellent for quadriceps complex (ICC = 0.85–0.99) and tibialis anterior (ICC = 0.93–0.99) measurements. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: ICC ranges and interpretation are the same; publication clarifies 'size and quality' but does not change the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictability of MyoVision-US for quadriceps complex and tibialis anterior parameters was moderate to strong, with adjusted R-squared values ranging from 0.56 to 0.94 and 0.81 to 0.97, respectively. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The comparability of MyoVision-US with manual analysis is moderate to strong for quadriceps complex (adj. R2 = 0.56–0.94) and tibialis anterior parameters (adj. R2 = 0.81–0.97). (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Statistics and interpretation are preserved; wording shifts from 'predictability' to 'comparability' but meaning is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of AI in automating lower limb muscle ultrasound analyses demonstrates excellent consistency and strong predictability compared to human analysis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of AI in automating lower limb muscle ultrasound analyses demonstrates excellent consistency and strong comparability compared with human analysis across various populations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication adds scope ('across various populations') clarifying/generalizing the conclusion while preserving the original meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future work should explore AI-powered models for the evaluation of other skeletal muscle groups. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The forward-looking suggestion present in the preprint does not appear in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: aiChat's response was assessed as 'correct' for 180 out of 216 evaluated questions, indicating an accuracy rate of 83.3%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: aiChat's response was assessed as 'correct' for 180 out of 216 evaluated questions, indicating an accuracy rate of 83.3%. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For a subset of questions, 60 out of 162 references provided in the aiChat summaries were confirmed as nonfabricated, representing 37%. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Of the 162 references provided in the aiChat summaries, 60 (37%) were confirmed as nonfabricated. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No significant differences were observed in question ratings by question category (p=0.39). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: No significant differences were observed in question ratings by question category (p=0.73). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core claim (no significant differences) remains but the reported p-value changed between preprint and publication, indicating a revision of the quantitative result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of a generative AI tool was promising, but many included references could not be independently verified. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of a generative AI tool was promising, but many included references could not be independently verified. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion and caveat are preserved unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study is envisioned as the first of a series of investigations to understand the integration of generative AI into medical librarians' workflow. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study is envisioned as the first of a series of investigations to understand the integration of generative AI into medical librarians' workflow. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The stated forward-looking contribution is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models fitted to a large longitudinal dataset can predict individual electrode thresholds and deactivation based on various predictors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our models enabled predictions of whether an electrode was deactivated in a given trial with F1 and area under the ROC curve scores of up to 0.732 and 0.911, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models accounted for up to 77% of the perceptual threshold response variance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our models accounted for up to 76% of the perceptual threshold response variance. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deactivation and threshold models identified novel predictors of perceptual sensitivity, including subject age, time since blindness onset, and electrode-fovea distance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our models identified novel predictors of perceptual sensitivity, including subject age, time since blindness onset, and electrode-fovea distance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The predictors identified are the same between preprint and publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Routinely collected clinical measures and a single session of system fitting might be sufficient to inform an XAI-based threshold prediction strategy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results demonstrate that routinely collected clinical measures and a single session of system fitting might be sufficient to inform an XAI-based threshold prediction strategy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT did not perform significantly differently than human respondents in answering genetics questions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT did not perform significantly differently than human respondents in answering questions about human genetics. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT performed significantly better on memorization-type questions compared to critical thinking questions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT frequently provided different answers when asked the same questions multiple times. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT frequently provided different answers when asked the same question multiple times, including for both initially correct and incorrect answers. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT provided plausible explanations for both correct and incorrect answers. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT was 68.2% accurate, compared to 66.6% accuracy for human respondents. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces explicit accuracy percentages that were not present in the preprint's listed claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT currently demonstrates significant shortcomings for clinical or other high-stakes use. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The published paper adds an explicit limitation about high-stakes use that was not stated among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model achieved an area under receiving operating characteristic (AUROC) of 0.97 and an area under precision-recall curve (AUPRC) of 0.97 in detecting HFrEF on the held-out set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieved an area under receiver-operating characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.97 and area under precision recall curve (AUPRC) of 0.97 in detecting HFrEF on the held-out set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numbers and evaluation context are identical between preprint and publication, indicating no substantive change in the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In external validation, the model had high performance in identifying HFrEF from discharge summaries with AUROC 0.94 and AUPRC 0.91. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model had high performance in identifying HFrEF with AUROC values ranging from 0.91 to 0.95 across different validation datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a range across multiple validation sets rather than a single external set; the core performance claim is consistent but generalized to multiple datasets.
The numeric NRI and comparison (vs diagnosis codes) are identical between preprint and publication, showing no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We developed and externally validated a deep learning language model that automatically identifies HFrEF from clinical notes with high precision and accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The authors developed a novel deep-learning language model for identifying patients with HFrEF from discharge summaries. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both texts describe development and validation of a language model to identify HFrEF from clinical notes/discharge summaries; the publication frames it as the paper's contribution but retains the same core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The final Discriminative classification model achieved an accuracy of 0.977 in the hold-out test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The final Discriminative classification model achieved an accuracy of 0.977 in the hold-out test set. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrated a sensitivity of 1.0 for identifying confirmed cases of opioid-induced respiratory depression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model demonstrated a sensitivity of 1.0 for identifying confirmed cases of opioid-induced respiratory depression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The sensitivity claim is reported identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Application of a data programming paradigm with expert-informed labeling functions might have utility for phenotyping clinical phenomena that are not easily ascertainable from highly-structured data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Application of a data programming paradigm with expert-informed labeling functions might have utility for phenotyping clinical phenomena that are not easily ascertainable from highly-structured data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach reduces the number of manual reviews needed by excluding visits/patients with low probabilities. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: All of the confirmed cases were identified by the classifier, which reduces the number of manual reviews needed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the reduction in manual review as a consequence of the classifier identifying all confirmed cases (stronger, result-oriented wording), increasing the certainty compared with the preprint's more tentative phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Federated models can achieve the same level of accuracy, precision, and generalizability as standard centralized statistical models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Federated models can achieve similar accuracy, precision, and generalizability as standard centralized statistical models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparative performance claim with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Federated learning techniques provide significantly stronger privacy protections compared to centralized data analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Federated learning techniques provide considerably stronger privacy protections without significantly raising computational costs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the privacy-strength claim but adds an explicit point about computational cost; the core claim about stronger privacy remains intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work is the first to apply modern and general federated learning methods to clinical and epidemiological research. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work is the first to apply modern federated learning methods that explicitly incorporate differential privacy to clinical and epidemiological research. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Health research participants can remain in control of their data while still contributing to advancing science. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Health research participants can remain in control of their data while still contributing to advancing science. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The participant-control conclusion is carried over verbatim and with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Damaging genotypes in chromatin-modifying and cilia-related genes were associated with an elevated risk of adverse post-operative outcomes, including mortality, cardiac arrest and prolonged mechanical ventilation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Damaging genotypes in chromatin-modifying and cilia-related genes are associated with an elevated risk of adverse post-operative outcomes, including mortality, cardiac arrest and prolonged mechanical ventilation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The association and the listed adverse outcomes are reported consistently between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The impact of damaging genotypes was further amplified in the context of specific CHD phenotypes, surgical complexity and extra-cardiac anomalies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The impact of damaging genotypes is further amplified in the context of specific CHD phenotypes, surgical complexity and extra-cardiac anomalies. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The absence of a damaging genotype in chromatin-modifying and cilia-related genes was also informative, reducing the risk for adverse postoperative outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The absence of a damaging genotype in chromatin-modifying and cilia-related genes is also informative, reducing the risk for some adverse postoperative outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Genome sequencing enriches the ability to forecast outcomes following congenital cardiac surgery. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Genome sequencing enriches the ability to forecast outcomes following congenital cardiac surgery. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the value of genome sequencing for outcome forecasting is carried forward without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HIPS-transformed images improved intra-thalamic contrast and thalamic boundaries. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HIPS-transformed images improved intra-thalamic contrast and thalamic boundaries. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HIPS-THOMAS yielded significantly higher mean Dice coefficients and reduced volume errors compared to both the CNN method and T1w-THOMAS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HIPS-THOMAS yielded significantly higher mean Dice coefficients and reduced volume errors compared to both the CNN method and T1w-THOMAS. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HIPS displayed the least inter-scanner variability and performed comparably with T1w-THOMAS for intra-scanner variability. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HIPS displayed the least inter-scanner variability and performed comparably with T1w-THOMAS for intra-scanner variability. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The variability comparisons across scanner contexts are preserved with similar certainty in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study introduces histogram-based polynomial synthesis (HIPS) as a preprocessing transform step for synthesizing WMn-like image contrast from standard T1w MRI. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HIPS is effective in enhancing thalamic nuclei segmentation from standard T1w MRI. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized the method introduction, while the publication states effectiveness for segmentation, moving from a contribution description to a stronger, outcome-focused conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BrainAgeNeXt achieved a mean absolute error (MAE) of 2.78 ± 3.64 years, lower than the compared methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BrainAgeNeXt achieved a mean absolute error (MAE) of 2.78 ± 3.64 years, lower than the compared methods (MAE range 3.55-4.16 years). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In three longitudinal multiple sclerosis (MS) cohorts, brain age was, on average, 4.21 ± 6.51 years greater than chronological age. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In three longitudinal multiple sclerosis (MS) cohorts, brain age was, on average, 4.21 ± 6.51 years greater than chronological age. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Longitudinal analysis indicated that brain age increased by 1.15 years per chronological year in individuals with MS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Longitudinal analysis indicated that brain age increased by 1.15 years per chronological year in individuals with MS. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individuals with worsening disability in early MS had a higher annual increase in brain age compared to those with stable clinical assessments. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individuals with worsening disability in early MS had a higher annual increase in brain age compared to those with stable clinical assessments. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings suggest that brain age is a promising prognostic biomarker for MS progression. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The artificial intelligence model built using a convolutional neural network can distinguish diseased from healthy samples and differentiate seven subtypes of mature B-cell neoplasm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The artificial intelligence model built can distinguish diseased from healthy samples and differentiate seven subtypes of mature B-cell neoplasm. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model was trained with 18,274 cases and achieved a weighted F1 score of 0.94 on a separate test set of 2,348 cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieved a weighted F1 score of 0.94 on a separate test set of 2,348 cases. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification model can classify 70% of all cases with a confidence of 0.95 and higher. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classification model can classify 70% of all cases with a confidence of 0.95 and higher. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Further improvement in performance is expected for rare subtypes when more samples are available for training. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fifteen drugs were significantly associated with decreased COVID-19 severity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Seventeen drugs were significantly associated with decreased COVID-19 severity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core claim (drugs associated with decreased severity) is retained but the reported count changed from 15 to 17, expanding the set of implicated drugs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Previous exposure to Streptococcus pneumoniae vaccines, diphtheria toxoid vaccine, and tetanus toxoid vaccine were significantly associated with a decreased risk of death from COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Previous exposure to two types of 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccines and diphtheria toxoid and tetanus toxoid vaccine were significantly associated with a decreased risk of death from COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same vaccine-related protective association but provides more specific vaccine nomenclature (two types of 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccines).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Secondary analyses identified several other significant associations showing lower risk for COVID-19 outcomes, including two vaccines and three dietary supplements. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several other significant associations were identified showing lower risk for COVID-19 outcomes, including various vaccines and supplements. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both describe additional protective associations; the publication uses broader wording (various vaccines and supplements) rather than enumerating exact counts.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This cohort study leveraged EHR data to identify a list of drugs that could be repurposed to improve COVID-19 outcomes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This cohort study leveraged EHR data to identify a list of drugs that could be repurposed to improve COVID-19 outcomes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The study purpose and conclusion about drug repurposing from EHR data are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further randomized clinical trials are needed to investigate the efficacy of the proposed drugs. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further randomized clinical trials are needed to investigate the efficacy of the proposed drugs. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation calling for randomized trials is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CG Signature achieves an Area Under the Receiver-Operating Characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.960±0.01 for binary classification and 0.771±0.024 to 0.904±0.012 for ternary classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CGSignature achieves outstanding model performance with an Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic curve of 0.960 ± 0.01 for binary classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the reported binary AUROC exactly but omits the ternary-range numbers in this claim, emphasizing the binary result; this clarifies focus rather than changing performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 'digital-grade' cancer staging produced by CG Signature significantly outperforms the AJCC 8th edition Tumor-Node-Metastasis staging system. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Kaplan–Meier survival analysis indicates that the 'digital grade' cancer staging produced by CGSignature significantly outperforms the AJCC 8th edition Tumor Node Metastasis staging system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The substantive claim of outperforming AJCC staging is the same; the publication frames it as demonstrated via Kaplan–Meier analysis, adding methodological clarity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Kaplan-Meier survival analysis indicates that CG Signature provides a remarkable capability in discriminating both binary and ternary classes with statistical significance (p-value < 0.0001). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Kaplan–Meier survival analysis indicates that the 'digital grade' cancer staging produced by CGSignature significantly outperforms the AJCC 8th edition Tumor Node Metastasis staging system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims concern Kaplan–Meier evidence of discriminative prognostic power; the publication emphasizes outperforming AJCC staging specifically, which clarifies the application of the KM result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CG Signature improves the assessment of the link between the TME spatial patterns and patient prognosis. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using Cell-Graphs extracted from mIHC images, CGSignature improves the assessment of the link between the TME spatial patterns and patient prognosis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same contribution and adds the method (Cell-Graphs from mIHC), adding detail that clarifies how the improvement is achieved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study suggests the feasibility and benefits of an artificial intelligence-powered digital staging system in diagnostic pathology and precision oncology. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study suggests the feasibility and benefits of an artificial intelligence-powered digital staging system in diagnostic pathology and precision oncology. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion about feasibility and benefits of AI-powered digital staging is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High-quality machine-learning models for 12 recurrent CH driver genes can identify their driver mutations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models trained for 12 recurrent CH genes can identify their driver mutations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about ML models identifying driver mutations across 12 CH genes is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine-learning models outperform an experimental base-editing approach and expert-curated rules. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These machine learning models outperform expert-curated rules based on prior knowledge of gene function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the outperforming of expert-curated rules but removes the explicit comparison to an experimental base-editing approach, narrowing the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of these models to almost half a million donors of the UK Biobank reproduces known associations between CH driver mutations and age, as well as the prevalence of several diseases and conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of these models to the UK Biobank data reproduces known associations between CH driver mutations and age, as well as the prevalence of several diseases and conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported reproduction of known associations using UK Biobank data is consistent between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These models support the accurate identification of CH across healthy individuals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed models support the accurate identification of CH across healthy individuals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion that models support accurate CH identification in healthy individuals is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Majority Weighted Vote and Dynamic Model Selection methods demonstrate superior performance compared to individual LLMs across three medical QA datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both ensemble methods outperformed individual LLMs across all 3 medical QA datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion that both ensemble methods outperform individual LLMs across the three datasets with no substantive change in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracies for the Majority Weighted Vote method are 35.84%, 96.21%, and 37.26% for MedMCQA, PubMedQA, and MedQA-USMLE, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Boosting-based Majority Weighted Vote achieved accuracies of 35.84% on MedMCQA, 96.21% on PubMedQA, and 37.26% on MedQA-USMLE.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the identical numeric accuracies for the Majority Weighted Vote method, so the result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Dynamic Model Selection yields slightly higher accuracies of 38.01%, 96.36%, and 38.13% for MedMCQA, PubMedQA, and MedQA-USMLE, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the specific Dynamic Model Selection numeric results present in the preprint (it keeps a general statement about ensembles), so the specific numeric claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The LLM-Synergy framework with two ensemble methods represents a significant advancement in leveraging LLMs for medical QA tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The LLM-Synergy framework represents a significant advancement in leveraging LLMs for medical QA tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion but presents it with higher certainty (publication lists the claim as high certainty versus medium in the preprint), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TR-OCTAs showed high image quality in both 3 and 6 mm datasets compared to GT-OCTAs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TR-OCTAs showed high image quality in both 3 and 6 mm datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result about high image quality for both scan sizes with essentially the same meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There were slight discrepancies in vascular metrics, especially in diseased patients.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly report the preprint's claim about discrepancies particularly in diseased patients; instead it introduces other validation details, so the specific claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Blood vessel features like tortuosity and vessel perimeter index showed a better trend compared to density features.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Vascular features like tortuosity and vessel perimeter index exhibit more consistent trends compared to density features.
alignments.rationale: The publication uses slightly different wording ('more consistent trends') but conveys the same comparative finding about vessel features, clarifying the phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study presents a promising solution to the limitations of OCTA adoption in clinical practice.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study presents a promising solution to the limitations of OCTA adoption in clinical practice.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about the study offering a promising solution for OCTA adoption with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study has the potential to significantly enhance the diagnostic process for retinal diseases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research has the potential to significantly enhance the diagnostic process for retinal diseases.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same claim about potential to improve diagnostic processes for retinal disease without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our multimodal model achieved a lead time of at least 12 hours ahead of clinical manifestation for predicting AKI and CRRT.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our multimodal model achieved a lead time of at least 12 hours ahead of clinical manifestation for predicting AKI and CRRT.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the identical result about lead time with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUROC) for AKI was 0.888 and for CRRT was 0.997, indicating high predictive performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUROC) for AKI was 0.888 and for CRRT was 0.997, indicating strong predictive performance.
alignments.rationale: The numeric AUROC values and interpretation are preserved; wording changed only minimally ('high' to 'strong').

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model significantly outperformed baseline models in predicting AKI and CRRT.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model significantly outperformed baseline models in predicting AKI and CRRT.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same comparative performance claim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our study revealed the importance of applying longitudinal, multimodal modeling to improve early prediction of AKI and CRRT.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our study revealed the importance of applying longitudinal, multimodal modeling to improve early prediction of AKI and CRRT.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same methodological conclusion about longitudinal multimodal modeling.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance and interpretability of our model indicate its potential for further assessment towards clinical applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance and interpretability of our model indicate its potential for further assessment towards clinical applications.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about potential for clinical assessment without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The stringent quarantine, massive lockdown, and other public health measures imposed in late January significantly reduced the transmission rate of COVID-19.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stringent quarantines, city lockdowns, and local public health measures imposed in late January significantly decreased the virus transmission rate.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same finding about late-January public health measures substantially reducing transmission with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: By early February, the virus spread had been contained.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The virus spread was contained by the middle of February.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts the containment timing from 'early February' to 'middle of February', a temporal revision of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A robust government response since late January played a determinant role in the containment of the virus.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Over 1.4 million infections and 56,000 deaths may have been avoided as a result of the national and provincial public health measures imposed in late January in China.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides quantitative estimates of avoidable infections and deaths attributable to the measures, clarifying and strengthening the preprint's attribution of containment to the government response.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The actual population flow from the outbreak source poses a higher risk to the destination than other factors such as geographic proximity and similarity in economic conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Population outflow from the outbreak source region posed a higher risk to the destination regions than other factors, including geographic proximity and similarity in economic conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same comparative-risk finding but lists it with somewhat lower certainty (publication labels it medium rather than high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Contextual determinants of health are associated with the spread of covid-19.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A relevant constellation of contextual factors of disease spread was identified using random forest machine learning methodology.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the association more explicit and adds methodological detail (random forest identification), thereby clarifying the original general claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A relevant constellation of contextual factors influencing disease spread was identified using random forest machine learning methodology.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A relevant constellation of contextual factors of disease spread was identified using random forest machine learning methodology.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same methodological result about identifying contextual factors with the same wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The impact of contextual factors on disease spread manifests differently for urban and rural counties.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The contextual factors of disease spread manifest differently for urban and rural counties.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same finding about differential manifestation of contextual factors across urban and rural counties.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings have clear implications for better preparing for future waves of disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings have clear implications for better preparing for the next wave of disease.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same implication about preparedness for future/next waves with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We mapped 219 molecular features with high significance to COVID-19 status and severity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We mapped 219 molecular features with high significance to COVID-19 status and severity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same numeric result and interpretation verbatim, so there is no change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The observed dysregulation of platelet function, blood coagulation, acute phase response, and endotheliopathy further illuminated the unique COVID-19 phenotype. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The observed dysregulation of platelet function, blood coagulation, acute phase response, and endotheliopathy further illuminated the unique COVID-19 phenotype. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion appears identical in wording and emphasis between preprint and publication, so no semantic, scope, or confidence change is evident.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified sets of covarying molecules, offering pathophysiological insights and therapeutic suggestions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified sets of covarying molecules, offering pathophysiological insights and therapeutic suggestions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result and its implications are presented the same way in both versions, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present a web-based tool (covid-omics.app) enabling interactive exploration of our compendium. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We present a web-based tool (covid-omics.app) enabling interactive exploration of our compendium. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution (web tool) is described identically in the publication, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Antibody-mediated phagocytosis, cytotoxicity, complement deposition, and neutralization were accurately predicted from biophysical antibody profiles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Antibody-mediated phagocytosis, cytotoxicity, complement deposition, and neutralization were accurately predicted from biophysical antibody profiles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same set of predicted functions and source data, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SARS-CoV-2-specific IgM was identified as a key predictor of neutralization activity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SARS-CoV-2-specific IgM was identified as a key predictor of neutralization activity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The identification of IgM as a key predictor is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mechanistic relevance of SARS-CoV-2-specific IgM as a predictor of neutralization activity was supported experimentally by depletion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mechanistic relevance of SARS-CoV-2-specific IgM as a predictor of neutralization activity was supported experimentally by depletion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions report the same experimental depletion evidence supporting IgM's mechanistic relevance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Validated models suggest desirable attributes of the humoral response to recapitulate by vaccination or other antibody-based interventions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Validated models suggest desirable attributes to recapitulate by vaccination or other antibody-based interventions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Wording is slightly condensed in the publication but the substantive conclusion about attributes to target by vaccination/antibody interventions is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dimensional neuroimaging endophenotypes (DNEs) capture the degree of expression of respective neuroanatomical patterns. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The nine dimensional neuroimaging endophenotypes (DNEs) capture the degree of expression of respective neuroanatomical patterns. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the claim by specifying that there are nine DNEs, narrowing the original more general statement to a defined set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presence of nine DNEs is associated with 31 genomic loci linked to Alzheimer's disease, autism spectrum disorder, late-life depression, and schizophrenia. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Phenome-wide associations align with genome-wide associations, revealing 31 genomic loci associated with the nine DNEs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements report 31 loci associated with the nine DNEs; the publication frames this in terms of alignment of PheWAS/GWAS rather than listing specific disorders, so the core result is clarified but not materially changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The nine DNEs significantly enhance the predictive accuracy for 14 systemic disease categories, especially for mental health and central nervous system conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The nine DNEs significantly enhanced the predictive accuracy for 14 systemic disease categories, particularly for conditions related to mental health and the central nervous system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same result about predictive improvement for 14 disease categories, with identical emphasis on mental health/CNS conditions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest that the nine DNEs can relate measures of disease-related brain phenotypes to genetics, lifestyle factors, and chronic diseases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings underscore the potential of the nine DNEs to capture the expression of disease-related brain phenotypes in individuals of the general population. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the conclusion toward population-level capture of disease-related brain phenotype expression, which broadens the emphasis from specific relations (genetics, lifestyle, chronic disease) to general population applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm reduced the number of required patient features to 7 inputs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm reduces the number of required patient features to seven inputs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is restated in the publication with the same numeric claim and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of MLP using 12 features is 0.903, while the accuracy of the proposed MLP with the TLBO method is 0.891. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of MLP using 12 features is 0.903, while that of the proposed MLP with TLBO is 0.891. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric accuracies and comparison between models are identical between versions, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The diagnostic accuracy in all methods, except the model designed with the Bayesian Network, increased when the SMOTE balancer was applied. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The diagnostic accuracy of all methods, except the model designed with the Bayesian network, increases when the SMOTE balancer is applied. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same, but the publication elevates certainty (medium -> high) and uses present-tense phrasing, indicating amplified confidence in the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Decision tree deep learning methods showed the highest levels of accuracy with 12 features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The decision tree-based deep learning methods show the highest levels of accuracy with 12 features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about decision tree methods remains the same; changes are stylistic only.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model provided great diagnostic accuracy by reducing the required properties from the samples without reducing the accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model provides high diagnostic accuracy while reducing the required number of properties from the samples. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion in a more concise form, dropping the explicit 'without reducing the accuracy' phrasing but preserving the substantive claim about maintaining high accuracy while reducing features.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SARS-CoV-2 shows substantial sequence diversity in a community-based sample. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SARS-CoV-2 shows substantial sequence diversity in a community-based sample. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about substantial sequence diversity is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two dominant clades of virus are in circulation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two dominant clades of virus are in circulation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions report the same finding about two dominant circulating clades with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No significant difference in outcomes of hospitalization or death could be discerned between clades in this sample. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: No significant difference in outcomes of hospitalization or death could be discerned between clades in this sample. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The negative result about no discernible difference in hospitalization or death between clades is identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A trend toward higher rates of hospitalization of patients with Clade 2 was observed (p=0.06). (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A trend toward higher rates of hospitalization of patients with Clade 2 infections was observed. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the 'trend' observation for Clade 2 hospitalizations; it omits the explicit p-value but the substantive, non-significant trend language remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Major risk factors for hospitalization and death for either major clade of virus include patient age and comorbid conditions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Major risk factors for hospitalization and death for either major clade of virus include patient age and comorbid conditions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The risk-factor conclusion is identical in both preprint and publication, showing no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current suicide-related behavior (SRB) risk assessment methods treat SRB risk as static and are confined to specific times and locations. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate or discuss this limitation as an explicit claim; none of the Q claims correspond to this critique of prior methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Event-GRU-ODE and Event-GRU-Discretized models can predict the dynamic risk of suicide-related behaviors as a continuous trajectory. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Event-GRU-ODE and Event-GRU-Discretized models can predict the dynamic risk of suicide-related behaviors as a continuous trajectory across future time points. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same capability but adds the explicit phrase 'across future time points,' making the claim slightly more specific rather than changing its meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both models demonstrated high discrimination performance for SRB prediction, with an AUROC greater than 0.92 in the full, general cohort. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both models demonstrated high discrimination, with the Event-GRU-ODE achieving an AUROC of 0.93 and a relative risk of 13.4 at 95% specificity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a precise AUROC value (0.93) and additional metrics (relative risk), refining the preprint's 'greater than 0.92' statement without reversing it.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These models can update risk estimations as new data becomes available, addressing the time gaps in current SRB risk assessments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models can update risk estimates as new data become available, even without new observations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the update-as-data-arrive claim but extends it by asserting updates can occur 'even without new observations,' strengthening the original conclusion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This work provides an initial step toward developing novel suicide prevention strategies based on dynamic changes in risk. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a forward-looking contribution about prevention strategy development that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrated the utility of large language models (LLMs) for extracting UTI symptoms from unstructured emergency department clinical notes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrated the utility of large language models (LLMs) for extracting urinary tract infection (UTI) symptoms from unstructured emergency department clinical notes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication state the same overall conclusion about LLM utility for extracting UTI symptoms from ED notes with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The transformer-based model (Longformer) achieved an F1 measure of 0.98 for identifying the presence or absence of any UTI symptom in clinical notes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The transformer-based model (Clinical Longformer) achieved an F1 measure of 0.98 for identifying the presence or absence of any UTI symptom in clinical notes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported metric and model match exactly; the publication only slightly names the transformer as 'Clinical Longformer,' not changing the claim's substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The convolutional neural network (SpaCy) model achieved an F1 measure of 0.96 for identifying the presence or absence of any UTI symptom in clinical notes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The convolutional neural network-based model (SpaCy) achieved an F1 measure of 0.96 for identifying the presence or absence of any UTI symptom in clinical notes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim for the SpaCy model is repeated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Overall F1 measure for note-level symptom identification was 0.84 for the SpaCy model and 0.88 for the Longformer model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or restate these overall note-level F1 summary values; instead, it emphasizes variable performance for individual symptoms, so the specific overall-F1 claim is omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Models showed variable performance for individual symptoms when detecting UTI symptoms from clinical notes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a summary observation about variability across individual symptoms that was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ShapeMed-Knee is a 3D shape dataset that includes 9,376 high-resolution, medical-imaging-based 3D shapes of both femur bone and cartilage. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ShapeMed-Knee is a 3D shape dataset that includes 9,376 high-resolution, medical-imaging-based 3D shapes of both femur bone and cartilage. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The dataset description is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hybrid explicit-implicit neural shape model developed using ShapeMed-Knee achieves up to 40% better reconstruction accuracy than a statistical shape model and two implicit neural shape models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hybrid explicit-implicit neural shape model developed using ShapeMed-Knee achieves up to 40% better reconstruction accuracy than a statistical shape model and two implicit neural shape models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance improvement claim is restated without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hybrid models are the first to successfully predict localized structural features of osteoarthritis, achieving 63% accuracy in osteophyte size and localization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hybrid models are the first to successfully predict localized structural features of osteoarthritis, achieving 63% accuracy in osteophyte size and localization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the novel prediction and accuracy figures verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ShapeMed-Knee reduces barriers to applying 3D modeling in medicine and enhances the diagnosis and risk stratification for complex diseases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ShapeMed-Knee reduces barriers to applying 3D modeling in medicine and enhances diagnosis and risk stratification for complex diseases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about lowering barriers and enhancing clinical application is preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning models produced an accuracy of above 70% for the classification of healthy skin versus erythema. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning models produced an accuracy of above 70% for classifying healthy skin versus erythema. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The high-level accuracy claim is repeated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ensemble CNN model achieved an accuracy of 87%, sensitivity of 90%, and specificity of 82% for a 2-class problem. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ensemble CNN model achieved an accuracy of 87%, sensitivity of 90%, and specificity of 82% for a 2-class problem. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific performance metrics for the ensemble CNN are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study is the first to focus on erythema in radiation-dermatitis and produces benchmark results using machine learning models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study is the first to focus on erythema in radiation-dermatitis and produces benchmark results using machine learning models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The novelty and benchmark-contribution claim is carried over without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed system can act as a pre-screening and decision support tool for oncologists or patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed system can act as a pre-screening and decision support tool for oncologists or patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the suggested clinical utility as presented in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieved accuracies of 70.5% for seizures, 78.3% for aggression, 80.2% for self-injurious behavior, and 85.7% for elopement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieved accuracies of 70.5% for seizures, 78.3% for aggression, 80.2% for self-injurious behavior (SIB), and 85.7% for elopement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance results are reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High-risk behaviors can serve as early indicators of subsequent challenging behaviors and upcoming seizure events. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: High-risk behaviors can serve as early indicators of subsequent challenging behaviors and upcoming seizure events. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about high-risk behaviors as early indicators is present and unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first work to integrate the prediction of seizures with behavioral data in individuals with profound Autism Spectrum Disorder. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach expands the clinical utility of predictive modeling in Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication drops the explicit 'first to integrate' novelty framing and instead emphasizes broader clinical utility, which tempers the original novelty claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach expands the clinical utility of predictive modeling in Autism Spectrum Disorder. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach expands the clinical utility of predictive modeling in Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the claim that the approach expands clinical utility for ASD predictive modeling.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Early warning systems derived from these predictions can guide timely interventions and improve quality of life. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an application-oriented claim (early warning systems guiding interventions) that does not appear in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine learning algorithm was developed and validated to classify patients with post-acute sequelae of SARS CoV-2 (PASC), distinguishing between Multisystem Inflammatory Syndrome in Children (MIS-C) and non-MIS-C variants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning algorithm was developed and validated to classify patients with pediatric Post-Acute Sequelae of SARS CoV-2 (PASC).
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same core claim of developing and validating a pediatric PASC classification algorithm; wording is effectively unchanged aside from specifying 'pediatric' explicitly, which matches the preprint context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can identify PASC patients in health systems data with higher precision for inclusion in studies or higher recall for screening in clinical trials.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can identify PASC patients in health systems data with higher precision or higher recall depending on threshold settings.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same trade-off between precision and recall but adds the clarifying mechanism (threshold settings); publication certainty is somewhat lower (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Analysis of specific features contributing to the classification process may enhance understanding of features associated with PASC diagnoses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Analysis of specific features contributing to the classification process may enhance understanding of features associated with PASC diagnoses.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion verbatim with the same level of certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CluSA framework can accurately classify and predict the degree of kidney function at the biopsy and in one year without expert annotation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CluSA framework can accurately classify and predict the degree of kidney function at the biopsy and in one year without expert annotation.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and metrics reported in the publication; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For predicting high/low eGFR at the biopsy, the framework achieved a sensitivity of 0.97, specificity of 0.90, and accuracy of 0.95.
alignments.pub_claim_text: For predicting eGFR at the biopsy, the model achieved a sensitivity of 0.97, specificity of 0.90, and accuracy of 0.95.
alignments.rationale: Performance metrics are identical between preprint and publication for the biopsy eGFR prediction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For predicting eGFR changes in one year, the framework achieved a sensitivity of 0.83, specificity of 0.85, and accuracy of 0.84.
alignments.pub_claim_text: For predicting eGFR changes in one year, the model achieved a sensitivity of 0.83, specificity of 0.85, and accuracy of 0.84.
alignments.rationale: The one-year prediction performance metrics are reported unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study presents the first spatial analysis based on unsupervised machine learning algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study presents the first spatial analysis based on unsupervised machine learning algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is preserved verbatim from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CluSA framework can identify novel predictors of kidney function and renal prognosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CluSA framework can identify novel predictors of kidney function and renal prognosis.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion about identifying novel predictors is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The authors enhanced the accuracy of an automated landmark predicting system utilizing multi-phase deep learning and voting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The authors enhanced the accuracy of an automated landmark predicting system utilizing multi-phase deep learning and voting.
alignments.rationale: Claim about improved accuracy using multi-phase DL and voting is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Successful detection rates improved as the phase advances in the multi-phase deep learning system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Successful detection rates improved as the phase advances in the multi-phase deep learning system.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same phased improvement in detection rates.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed system marked better results compared to previously reported benchmarks using the same dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed system marked better results in comparison with previously reported benchmarks using the same dataset.
alignments.rationale: Benchmark comparisons and superior results are preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multi-phase deep learning may be a solution to deal with large images within the physical limitation of memory and computation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multi-phase deep learning may be a solution to deal with large images within the physical limitation of memory and computation.
alignments.rationale: The concluding proposition about multi-phase DL as a solution is carried forward without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The architecture with the highest out-of-training set accuracy was ResNet-101 at 97.5%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The architecture with the highest out-of-training set accuracy was ResNet-101 at 97.5%.
alignments.rationale: The top-performing architecture and reported accuracy remain identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural network training time varied between 2 to 7 hours depending on the depth of the neural network architecture.
alignments.rationale: Training-time detail reported in the preprint is not present among the publication claims, so it appears to have been removed from the main claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most commonly misclassified hearing loss type were mixed losses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The most commonly misclassified hearing loss type were mixed losses.
alignments.rationale: The misclassification finding is retained but the publication reports it with higher certainty, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Re-engineering the process of hearing testing with a machine learning innovation may help enhance access to the growing worldwide population that is expected to require audiologist services.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Re-engineering the process of hearing testing with a machine learning innovation may help enhance access to the growing worldwide population that is expected to require audiologist services.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is preserved but reported with increased certainty in the publication (medium vs low), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results suggest that deep learning may be a transformative technology that enables automatic and accurate audiogram interpretation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results suggest that deep learning may be a transformative technology that enables automatic and accurate audiogram interpretation.
alignments.rationale: This concluding claim appears verbatim with similar low certainty in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Each neural network architecture produced misclassifications that arose from failures of the model to correctly label the audiogram with the appropriate hearing loss type.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an explicit, broader statement about model misclassifications that did not appear as a distinct claim in the preprint, so it is treated as an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The vision transformer model pre-trained on ImageNet and fine-tuned on ADC data with age fusion achieved the highest performance in classifying pediatric brain tumors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The highest tumor-type classification performance was achieved using a vision transformer model pre-trained on ImageNet and fine-tuned on ADC images with age fusion.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same top-performing model configuration and result with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fusion of age information with the image data marginally improved classification performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Age fusion marginally improved the model's performance in classifying pediatric brain tumors.
alignments.rationale: The age-fusion effect is reported equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model architecture and pre-training strategies did not significantly impact the models’ performance.
