alignments.pub_claim_text: Both model architectures performed similarly across the experiments, with no differences between the pre-training strategies.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion about similar performance across architectures and pre-training strategies.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model explainability techniques showed that the models focus on tumor region information for classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Grad-CAMs indicated that the models' attention focused on the brain region during classification.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports attention on the 'brain region' rather than explicitly the 'tumor region'; this broadens the spatial focus and the claim is presented with slightly lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning-based classification of pediatric brain tumors can be achieved using single-sequence pre-operative MR data.
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about achieving classification from single-sequence pre-op MR data is not represented among the publication claims and appears to have been omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: PCA of the feature space showed greater separation of the tumor-type clusters when using contrastive pre-training.
alignments.rationale: The PCA/feature-space separation finding appears as a new result in the publication that was not present in the preprint, so it is treated as an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed platform based on a deep convolutional neural network (DCNN) can classify esophageal biopsies with an accuracy of 85%, sensitivity of 82.5%, and specificity of 87%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed platform based on a deep convolutional neural network (DCNN) can classify esophageal biopsies with an accuracy of 85%, sensitivity of 82.5%, and specificity of 87%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical performance metrics and wording between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The features contributing to the correct classification of esophageal biopsies are global rather than specific, local features. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The features contributing to the correct classification of esophageal biopsies are global rather than specific, local features. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim about global vs local features appears verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Artificial intelligence can identify eosinophilic esophagitis using computer vision analysis of esophageal biopsy slides. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Artificial intelligence can identify eosinophilic esophagitis (EoE) using computer vision analysis of esophageal biopsy slides. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same conclusion stated in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DCNN features associated with eosinophilic esophagitis are based on both local eosinophils and global histologic changes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DCNN features associated with EoE are based on both local eosinophils and global histologic changes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same content about both local and global features across preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The systematic analysis of image size versus downscaling tradeoff can improve disease classification performance in digital pathology. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This methodological claim about image-size/downscaling analysis does not appear in the publication; publication instead adds a different contribution (Q4).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The rule-based NLP algorithm achieved the best performance of F1 across all sleep-related concepts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The rule-based NLP algorithm achieved the best performance of F1 across all sleep-related concepts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical performance claim in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The rule-based NLP algorithm achieved a Positive Predictive Value (PPV) of 1.00 for daytime sleepiness and sleep duration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The rule-based NLP algorithm achieved the highest positive predictive value (PPV) scores for daytime sleepiness and sleep duration, both at 1.00. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same numeric PPV results reported in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed rule-based NLP algorithm and LLM-based NLP algorithms achieved promising results despite infrequent documentation of sleep information in clinical notes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study indicates that sleep information is infrequently documented in clinical notes, yet the proposed algorithms achieved promising results. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same overall conclusion about promising results despite infrequent documentation; wording slightly reordered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning-based approaches did not achieve good results due to the small size of sleep information in the training data. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Publication does not report or emphasize a claim that ML approaches failed for this reason; that limitation is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study's findings could be extended to general sleep information extraction for other diseases. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings suggest that the proposed methods for extracting sleep-related patterns could provide insight into the relationship between sleep and Alzheimer's disease incidence. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Preprint claimed broad generalizability; publication narrows to a specific application (Alzheimer's), representing a narrowing/revision of the claimed extension.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study achieved an accuracy of 0.81 using GPT-3.5 Turbo and an accuracy of 0.87 using GPT-4 Turbo. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study achieved an accuracy of 0.81 using GPT-3.5 Turbo and an accuracy of 0.87 using GPT-4. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance numbers are the same; publication slightly changes model naming (GPT-4 vs GPT-4 Turbo), a minor clarification in wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrated strong capabilities in identifying eligible patients and minimizing false inclusions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrated strong capabilities in identifying eligible patients and minimizing false inclusions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same claim present in publication, though labeled as a conclusion there.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings underscore the significant potential of AI and NLP technologies in the clinical trial matching process. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI and NLP technologies, including LLMs, have the potential to enhance the clinical trial matching process. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same high-level conclusion about potential of AI/NLP, though publication presents it with slightly lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated systems promise to greatly alleviate the workload of research staff and improve clinical trial enrollment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automated systems promise to alleviate the workload of research staff and improve clinical trial enrollment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication removes the modifier "greatly," making the claim more measured; content otherwise consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Remoscope performs label-free quantitative stage-specific detection of Plasmodium falciparum in 1-12 minutes without sample fixation, staining, or slide scanning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication does not restate the specific rapid, label-free, stage-specific detection time/processing claim present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Remoscope demonstrated a linear response across the entire range of parasite concentrations tested, from 17.1 to 710,000 parasites/μL. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the explicit linear-response across that full concentration range described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Remoscope's limit of detection with respect to qPCR was 95.1 parasites/μL, with a sensitivity of 83%, specificity of 96%, Positive Predictive Value of 91%, and Negative Predictive Value of 93%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Remoscope demonstrated a sensitivity of 83%, specificity of 96%, positive predictive value of 91%, and negative predictive value of 93%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance metrics (sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV) match directly; LOD is reported in the publication as well (Q0) though the metrics align most closely with Q1.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The half-maximal effective concentration of chloroquine for the W2 strain of Plasmodium falciparum was 211 nM by Remoscope, compared to 191 nM for conventional flow cytometry. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication does not include the chloroquine EC50 comparison reported in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Remoscope's speed, accuracy, low cost, and ease of use address practical challenges in malaria diagnostic settings around the world. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Remoscope's speed, accuracy, cost and ease of use address key challenges in malaria diagnosis worldwide. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same conclusion about addressing diagnostic challenges; wording slightly different but meaning unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We introduced novel AI-derived (AID) markers for clinical decision support using multimodal real-world data and explainable artificial intelligence. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI-derived (AID) markers were introduced for clinical decision support by combining multimodal real-world data and explainable artificial intelligence (xAI). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution claim present in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: xAI identified 114 key markers that accounted for 90% of the neural network’s decision process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: xAI identified 114 key markers that accounted for 90% of the neural network’s decision process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same numeric result reported in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: xAI uncovered 1,373 prognostic interactions between markers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: xAI uncovered 1,373 prognostic interactions between markers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical result in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach was validated in an independent cohort of 3,288 lung cancer patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach was validated in an independent cohort of 3,288 patients with lung cancer. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Validation cohort size and claim are identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These results show the potential of xAI to transform the assessment of clinical parameters and enable personalized, data-driven cancer care. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results show the potential of xAI to transform the assessment of clinical variables and enable personalized, data-driven cancer care. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same concluding statement about potential impact in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI-based image analysis platform demonstrated high classification accuracy for small bowel enteropathies, achieving 98% case-detection accuracy with ResNet50 and 96% with Shallow CNN.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI-based image analysis platform demonstrated high classification accuracy for small bowel enteropathies.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same high-level claim about high classification accuracy; the preprint's numeric specifics are compatible with the publication's broader statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification accuracy increased to 98.3% when using an ensemble of the models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ResNet50 and shallow CNN achieved case-detection accuracies of 98% and 96%, respectively, which increased to 98.3% with an ensemble.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact numeric performance and the ensemble improvement stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Grad-CAMs illuminated the decision-making process of the deep learning models, enhancing physician confidence in adopting these technologies in clinical practice.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of Grad-CAMs illuminated the decision-making process of deep learning models, increasing physician confidence in adopting these technologies.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the same effect of Grad-CAMs on model interpretability and physician confidence with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI-based platform is capable of identifying biologically relevant microscopic features, emulating the decision-making process of human pathologists.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Grad-CAMs demonstrated the models' ability to learn different microscopic morphological features for environmental enteropathy, celiac disease, and controls.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states that Grad-CAMs reveal learned microscopic features, which matches the preprint claim that the platform identifies biologically relevant microscopic features.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The basic reproduction number of COVID-19 during the early outbreak was 4.22±1.69.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The basic reproduction number of COVID-19 during the early outbreak was 4.22 ± 1.69.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numeric R0 estimate as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: By May 10, 2020, the reproduction number dropped to 0.67±0.18.
alignments.pub_claim_text: By May 10, 2020, the reproduction number dropped to 0.67 ± 0.18.
alignments.rationale: Identical temporal reproduction number estimate is reported in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a strong correlation between passenger air travel, driving, walking, and transit mobility and the effective reproduction number with a time delay of 17.24±2.00 days.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a strong correlation between passenger air travel, driving, walking, and transit mobility and the effective reproduction number with a time delay of 17.24 ± 2.00 days.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same mobility–Re relation and time delay as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed dynamic SEIR model can simulate various outbreak control and exit strategies to inform political decision making.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed dynamic SEIR model can simulate various outbreak control and exit strategies to inform political decision making.
alignments.rationale: The contribution of the model is presented identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: COVID-19 was characterized by a diminished innate immune response compared to other viral ARIs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: COVID-19 is characterized by a pronounced interferon response but attenuated activation of other innate immune pathways compared to other viral acute respiratory illnesses.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the preprint's generalized 'diminished innate response' by specifying that interferon responses are pronounced while other innate pathways are attenuated, narrowing the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with COVID-19 exhibited significantly reduced proportions of neutrophils and macrophages, and increased proportions of goblet, dendritic and B-cells compared to other viral ARIs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The IL-1 and NLRP3 inflammasome pathways are markedly less responsive to SARS-CoV-2, which is associated with diminished neutrophil and macrophage recruitment.
alignments.rationale: The publication links decreased neutrophil/macrophage presence to specific inflammasome pathway attenuation; it omits some cell-type increases reported in the preprint, focusing the biological mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We built 26-, 10- and 3-gene classifiers that differentiated COVID-19 from other acute respiratory illnesses with AUCs of 0.980, 0.950 and 0.871, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning classifiers can differentiate COVID-19 from other acute respiratory illnesses with high accuracy, achieving AUROCs of 0.981, 0.954, and 0.885.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports essentially the same classifier result with minor numeric adjustments to AUROC values; this is a clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Classifier performance was stable at low viral loads, suggesting utility in settings where direct detection of viral nucleic acid may be unsuccessful.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Classifier performance is stable across a wide range of viral load, suggesting utility in mitigating false positive or false negative results of direct SARS-CoV-2 tests.
alignments.rationale: The publication broadens the claim from stability at low viral loads to stability across a wide range and explicitly emphasizes practical utility for mitigating test errors, strengthening the implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results illuminate unique aspects of the host transcriptional response to SARS-CoV-2 in comparison to other respiratory viruses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: COVID-19 is characterized by a pronounced interferon response but attenuated activation of other innate immune pathways compared to other viral acute respiratory illnesses.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a more specific characterization of the 'unique aspects' mentioned in the preprint (i.e., interferon vs other innate pathways), refining the original general statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simple stratification approaches for type 2 diabetes have been widely used but lack replication and meaningful outcome associations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many simple stratification approaches for type 2 diabetes have not been replicated and are not associated with meaningful clinical outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint finding about lack of replication and outcome association for simple approaches.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Complex stratification using clustering of clinical and genetic data shows reproducible subtypes of diabetes associated with outcomes like cardiovascular disease and mortality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Complex stratification approaches produce reproducible subtypes of type 2 diabetes that are associated with outcomes.
alignments.rationale: Both versions report reproducible subtypes from complex stratification that relate to clinical outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both simple and complex subclassification approaches require a higher grade of evidence to support their effectiveness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both simple and complex subclassification approaches require a higher grade of evidence to support their clinical relevance.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries the same cautionary conclusion about the need for stronger evidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: More studies are needed to test diabetes subclassifications in diverse ancestries and to confirm their amenability to interventions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a need to test whether subtypes of type 2 diabetes exist in more diverse ancestries.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the need to test subtypes across diverse ancestries but does not explicitly repeat the preprint's additional point about amenability to interventions, narrowing the focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictive model developed demonstrates 93% overall accuracy in predicting the mortality rate of COVID-19 patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive model developed demonstrates 89.98% overall accuracy in predicting the mortality rate of COVID-19 patients.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a lower overall accuracy than the preprint (93% → 89.98%), a numeric revision that slightly tempers the claimed model performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI model is proposed to assist hospitals in prioritizing patient care during overcrowding situations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI model proposed can help hospitals prioritize patients for hospitalization and triage during overcrowding.
alignments.rationale: Both versions present the same proposed application for hospital prioritization and triage.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several machine learning algorithms were utilized to predict the mortality rate in patients with COVID-19.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several machine learning algorithms were utilized to predict the mortality rate in patients with COVID-19.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the methodological claim but downgrades the certainty label (preprint high → publication medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identifies the most alarming symptoms and features associated with COVID-19.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identified the most alarming symptoms and features associated with COVID-19.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the finding about identified alarming symptoms and features with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neuropsychiatric symptoms (NPS) are highly prevalent (≥ 90%) in both early and late stages of behavioral variant frontotemporal dementia (bvFTD).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neuropsychiatric symptoms (NPS) were highly prevalent (≥90%) in early and late bvFTD.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same prevalence finding with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Four distinct clusters of NPS were identified in bvFTD: affective, disinhibited, compulsive, and psychosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four NPS clusters were identified based on magnitude of factor loadings: affective, disinhibited, compulsive, and psychosis.
alignments.rationale: The clusters are the same but the publication adds methodological detail (factor loadings), refining rather than changing the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Symptoms in the psychosis and compulsive clusters are largely stable, persisting from visit-to-visit in more than 50% of cases.
alignments.rationale: The stability claim about psychosis and compulsive clusters does not appear in the publication's listed claims and thus seems omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fluctuating nature of NPS in bvFTD suggests that they are not reliable markers of disease progression or stage.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fluctuating nature of some NPS in bvFTD suggests that they may not be reliable markers of disease progression or stage.
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the conclusion slightly ("may not" vs "are not") while preserving the overall claim about unreliability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified NPS clusters may result from specific neural network disruptions that could serve as targets for future interventions.
alignments.rationale: The mechanistic/targeting claim about neural network disruptions is not represented among the publication claims and appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher sCD14 and TNF-α concentrations were significantly associated with lower social perception scores.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Higher sCD14 and TNF-α concentrations were significantly associated with lower social perception scores.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Every 50% increase in the inflammation summary score was associated with a significantly lower Social Perception score.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Every 50% increase in the inflammation summary score was associated with a significantly lower Social Perception score.
alignments.rationale: The numeric association and interpretation are unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A unit increase in inflammatory composites of seven markers were associated with lower scores in language, visuospatial processing, and social perception domains.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A greater inflammatory composite variable from seven markers was associated with lower scores in language, visuospatial processing, and social perception domains.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same multi-domain association using slightly different wording ("greater composite" rather than "unit increase"), clarifying the metric.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher levels of inflammation were associated with lower neurobehavioral performance in adolescents, especially with social perception.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Higher levels of inflammation were associated with lower neurobehavioral performance in adolescents, especially with social perception.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is preserved verbatim between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a robust analytic method to examine an association between a composite inflammatory variable integrating seven markers led to additional findings in language and visuospatial processing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using a robust analytic method to examine an association between a composite inflammatory variable integrating seven markers led to additional findings in language and visuospatial processing.
alignments.rationale: The methodological/conclusion claim is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CMA had the highest prediction performance for cardiac sarcoidosis with an AUC of 0.919.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CMA had the highest prediction performance for cardiac sarcoidosis with an AUC of .919.
alignments.rationale: Numeric performance metric and interpretation are identical in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Abnormal CMA (≥1) had a sensitivity of 100% and specificity of 65% for cardiac sarcoidosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Abnormal CMA (≥1) had a sensitivity of 100% and specificity of 65% for cardiac sarcoidosis.
alignments.rationale: Diagnostic performance metrics are unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fully automated volumetric quantification of [18F]FDG PET for cardiac sarcoidosis is feasible and rapid.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fully automated volumetric quantification of [18F]FDG PET for cardiac sarcoidosis is feasible and rapid.
alignments.rationale: Feasibility and rapidity claim is directly preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lung quantification was able to identify patients with pulmonary abnormalities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lung quantification was able to identify patients with pulmonary abnormalities.
alignments.rationale: The lung-quantification result is present and unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Upon full reopening, every other day, a new COVID-19 case would enter the province.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Upon full reopening, every other day, a new COVID-19 case would enter the province.
alignments.rationale: The projected importation rate claim is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Banning air travel from outside Canada is more efficient in managing the pandemic than fully reopening and quarantining 95% of the incoming population.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Banning air travel from outside Canada is more efficient in managing the pandemic than fully reopening and quarantining 95% of the incoming population.
alignments.rationale: The comparative policy conclusion is maintained without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides quantitative insights of the efficacy of travel restrictions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides quantitative insights of the efficacy of travel restrictions.
alignments.rationale: The stated contribution is the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A prominent signature of neutrophil activation, including resistin, lipocalin-2, HGF, IL-8, and G-CSF, is identified as the strongest predictor of critical illness in COVID-19 patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A prominent signature of neutrophil activation, including resistin, lipocalin-2, hepatocyte growth factor, interleukin-8, and granulocyte colony-stimulating factor, were the strongest predictors of critical illness in COVID-19 patients.
alignments.rationale: The result listing the same neutrophil markers as top predictors is preserved; only minor naming expansions occurred.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neutrophil activation is present on the first day of hospitalization in patients who will later require transfer to the intensive care unit, indicating it precedes the onset of critical illness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evidence of neutrophil activation was present on the first day of hospitalization in patients who would later require transfer to the intensive care unit.
alignments.rationale: The temporal presence of neutrophil activation preceding ICU transfer is reported equivalently.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Early elevations in developing and mature neutrophil counts predict higher mortality rates in hospitalized COVID-19 patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Early elevations in developing and mature neutrophil counts predicted higher mortality rates in hospitalized COVID-19 patients.
alignments.rationale: The prognostic association with early neutrophil elevations is maintained, with only minor tense adjustment.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study defines an essential role for neutrophil activation in the pathogenesis of severe COVID-19.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These data suggest a central role for neutrophil activation in the pathogenesis of severe COVID-19.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's stronger wording ('defines an essential role') is softened in the publication to 'suggest a central role', indicating reduced claim strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Molecular neutrophil markers are identified that distinguish patients at risk of future clinical decompensation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Molecular markers identified in this study can distinguish patients at risk of future clinical decompensation.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the marker-discrimination claim but uses more cautious language and drops the explicit 'neutrophil' qualifier while lowering certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SARS-CoV-2 infection was associated with a marked increase in individual's immune memory antibody repertoires linked to trajectories of disease severity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SARS-CoV-2 infection was associated with a marked increase in immune antibody repertoires against many known pathogenic and non-pathogenic human viruses.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the reported marked increase but adds specificity that the increased repertoires are against many known pathogenic and non-pathogenic human viruses, expanding the scope of the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine-learning-based strategy was developed to create a viral exposure signature predictive of COVID-19-related disease severity linked to patient survival.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A viral exposure signature predictive of COVID-19-related disease severity linked to patient survival was developed and validated.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same machine-learning-derived signature but adds validation, which strengthens the claim and increases confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides a basis for understanding the roles of memory B-cell repertoires in COVID-19-related symptoms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A unique anti-viral antibody repertoire signature may be useful to define COVID-19 clinical severity.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's broader mechanistic framing about memory B-cell roles is shifted in the publication to a more cautious, applied statement about an antibody repertoire signature useful for defining clinical severity, narrowing scope and softening inference.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The levels of 25 metabolites measured at the time of hospital admission successfully predict future disease severity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A panel of metabolites measured at the time of study entry successfully determines disease severity in COVID-19 patients.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about a metabolite panel predicting disease severity with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prognostic markers identified are directly related to disease progression and their levels are restored to baseline upon disease recovery.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most of the identified metabolic markers are directly related to disease progression and their levels return to baseline upon disease recovery.
alignments.rationale: The publication echoes the preprint's claim that markers relate to progression and return to baseline, with similar strength and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Metabolic changes associated with COVID-19 severity can be effectively used to stratify patients and inform resource allocation during the pandemic.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A panel of metabolites measured at the time of study entry successfully determines disease severity in COVID-19 patients.
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms metabolites can stratify severity but does not make the operational claim about informing resource allocation; thus the claim is narrowed and framed more conservatively.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The metabolites identified are also altered in a hamster model of COVID-19.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The metabolites identified in the study are also altered in a hamster model of COVID-19.
alignments.rationale: The publication directly reproduces the preprint result about concordant metabolite changes in a hamster model.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High levels of circulating, enzymatically active secreted phospholipase A2 Group IIA (sPLA2-IIA) are present in severe and fatal COVID-19 disease compared with uninfected patients or those with mild illness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deceased COVID-19 patients had higher levels of circulating, catalytically active sPLA2 group IIA (sPLA2-IIA), with a median value that was 9.6-fold higher than that for patients with mild disease.
alignments.rationale: The publication corroborates the preprint's finding and adds quantitative detail (fold-change), clarifying the magnitude without changing the substantive result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning demonstrates that sPLA2-IIA effectively stratifies severe from fatal COVID-19 disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A decision tree generated by machine learning identified sPLA2-IIA levels as a central node in the stratification of patients who died from COVID-19.
alignments.rationale: Both claim that ML implicates sPLA2-IIA in stratification; the publication specifies a decision-tree central node, clarifying the ML method and role.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PLA-BUN index, which combines sPLA2-IIA and blood urea nitrogen (BUN) threshold levels, is a critical risk factor for mitochondrial dysfunction, sustained inflammatory injury, and lethal COVID-19.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combined PLA-BUN index performed significantly better than did either sPLA2-IIA or blood urea nitrogen (BUN) alone in predicting COVID-19 mortality.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports predictive performance of the combined index but does not support the broader mechanistic claims about mitochondrial dysfunction and sustained inflammatory injury, narrowing and tempering the original inference.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study opens the door to precision intervention using indices discovered here to reduce COVID-19 mortality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study identifies sPLA2-IIA as a therapeutic target to reduce COVID-19 mortality.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes a more definitive translational claim identifying sPLA2-IIA as a therapeutic target, which amplifies confidence relative to the preprint's more tentative suggestion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The crowdsourcing approach predicts preterm or early preterm birth from vaginal microbiome studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive models achieve area under the receiver operator characteristic (AUROC) curve scores of 0.69 for predicting preterm birth (PTB) and 0.87 for early preterm birth (ePTB).
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the performance metrics of the predictive models derived from the crowdsourcing approach, supporting the preprint result that the approach predicts PTB/ePTB.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Top-ranking submissions achieved bootstrapped AUROC scores of 0.69 and 0.87 in the prediction sub-challenges.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive models achieve area under the receiver operator characteristic (AUROC) curve scores of 0.69 for predicting preterm birth (PTB) and 0.87 for early preterm birth (ePTB).
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same AUROC performance figures for the top-performing submissions, matching the preprint claim directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Alpha diversity, VALENCIA community state types, and composition were important features in the top performing models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Alpha diversity, VALENCIA community state types, and composition are important features in the top-performing models.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly confirms the same feature importance reported in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work serves as the foundation for subsequent efforts to translate predictive tests into clinical practice.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work serves as a model for the translation of microbiome data into clinically relevant predictive models.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the work similarly as a model for translating microbiome analyses into clinically relevant predictive tools, matching the preprint's contribution claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Merging datasets from multiple studies improves the construction of gene expression-based prediction models for ICB therapy response.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Merging datasets from multiple studies and incorporating prior biological knowledge yielded prediction models with higher predictive accuracies.
alignments.rationale: The publication combines the merging-datasets claim with incorporation of prior biological knowledge; it therefore clarifies the preprint result by presenting both strategies together as yielding higher accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating prior biological knowledge yields prediction models with higher predictive accuracies for ICB therapy response.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Merging datasets from multiple studies and incorporating prior biological knowledge yielded prediction models with higher predictive accuracies.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly includes prior-knowledge incorporation as a contributor to higher predictive accuracy, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The development of gene expression signatures based on RNA sequencing data can contribute to more personalized treatment strategies for cancer patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Developing gene expression signatures based on RNA sequencing data and machine learning methods can predict a patient’s response to immune checkpoint blockade therapy.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the preprint's broad personalized-treatment claim to the specific application of predicting response to immune checkpoint blockade therapy, narrowing the claimed application while preserving the translational intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning tools in electronic health records can help reduce the underdiagnosis of dementia. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Race-specific machine learning models can help identify Black Americans who may have undiagnosed dementia. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a more specific application (race-specific models, focus on Black Americans) rather than the broader claim about EHR ML tools reducing underdiagnosis generally, so the claim is clarified and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There was a strong positive relationship between SVM-generated risk scores and undiagnosed dementia. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A strong positive relationship was observed between SVM-generated risk scores and undiagnosed dementia. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about the strong positive relationship between SVM risk scores and undiagnosed dementia with essentially identical meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Black Americans were more likely than white Americans to have undiagnosed dementia, both overall and among those with high-risk scores. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Black Americans were more likely than White Americans to have undiagnosed dementia per chart review, both overall and among Veterans with >90th percentile cutoff scores. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides the same finding, adding specific descriptors (chart review, >90th percentile) but preserving the core result and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The race-specific machine learning models performed differently, with the Black American model showing slightly better performance than the white American model. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Black American model performed slightly better than the White American model in identifying undiagnosed dementia. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same comparative performance between race-specific models with similar emphasis and magnitude.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Race-specific machine learning models can assist in the identification of undiagnosed dementia, particularly in Black Americans. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Race-specific machine learning models can help identify Black Americans who may have undiagnosed dementia. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same conclusion about the utility of race-specific models for identifying undiagnosed dementia in Black Americans with very similar wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SuStaIn identified two subtypes with distinct sequences of atrophy progression in cortico-basal syndrome (CBS). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Subtype and Stage Inference identified at least two distinct and longitudinally stable spatiotemporal subtypes of atrophy progression in corticobasal syndrome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes longitudinal stability and frames the finding as "at least two" spatiotemporal subtypes, which clarifies and slightly broadens the original two-subtype claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Four-repeat-tauopathy confirmed cases were most commonly assigned to the Subcortical subtype, while CBS-AD was most commonly assigned to the Fronto-parieto-occipital subtype. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four-repeat-tauopathy confirmed cases were most commonly assigned to the Subcortical subtype, while those with Alzheimer's pathology were most commonly assigned to the Fronto-parieto-occipital subtype. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same mapping between pathology-confirmed cases and subtypes, with matching meaning (CBS-AD described as Alzheimer's pathology).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Subtype assignment was stable at follow-up, with 98% of cases maintaining their subtype assignment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Subtype and Stage Inference identified at least two distinct and longitudinally stable spatiotemporal subtypes of atrophy progression in corticobasal syndrome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's characterization of subtypes as longitudinally stable aligns with the preprint's quantitative stability result, so the claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model supports the ability to stage progression, as 100% of individuals either stayed at the same stage or progressed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: By jointly modelling disease stage and subtype, the study provides data-driven evidence for distinct subtypes of atrophy in corticobasal syndrome that are associated with different underlying pathologies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the staging result as part of joint modelling that provides evidence linking subtypes to pathology; this clarifies the role of staging though it is less numeric than the preprint's "100%" phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accurately subtyping and staging individuals with CBS at baseline has important implications for screening in clinical trials and tracking disease progression. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Accurately subtyping and staging individuals with corticobasal syndrome at baseline has important implications for screening on entry into clinical trials and for tracking disease progression. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about implications for trial screening and tracking disease progression with nearly identical wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cohort included 29,168 individuals, with 2,703 (9.3%) meeting at least one criterion for postpartum depression in the 6 months following delivery. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claims do not restate the cohort counts or exact prevalence, so this specific descriptive claim appears omitted in the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The risk stratification model demonstrated good discrimination with an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.721. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve for the model was 0.721, indicating its effectiveness in predicting postpartum depression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication report the same AUC and interpret it as demonstrating effective discrimination.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: At a specificity of 90%, the positive predictive value of the model was 28.0% and the negative predictive value was 92.2%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: At a specificity of 90%, the model's positive predictive value was 28.8% and the negative predictive value was 92.2%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports essentially the same performance metrics but with a slightly different PPV (28.8% vs 28.0%), indicating a numeric revision while the overall claim remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings demonstrate that a simple machine-learning model can be used to stratify the risk for postpartum depression before delivery hospitalization discharge. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings suggest that the machine-learning model could facilitate individualized postpartum care planning for the prevention and management of postpartum depression. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the utility from risk stratification before discharge to facilitating individualized care planning (a broader application) and lowers certainty from high to medium, so the claim is clarified, broadened, and tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Low-frequency ANT-DBS showed greater reductions in seizures compared to high-frequency ANT-DBS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Continuous low-frequency ANT-DBS showed greater reductions in interictal epileptiform spikes, electrographic seizures, and better sleep and memory outcomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms greater benefit of low-frequency stimulation but provides more specific electrophysiologic and outcome detail, clarifying the original seizure-reduction claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Low-frequency ANT-DBS resulted in better sleep and memory outcomes than high-frequency ANT-DBS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Continuous low-frequency ANT-DBS showed greater reductions in interictal epileptiform spikes, electrographic seizures, and better sleep and memory outcomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same finding about improved sleep and memory outcomes with continuous low-frequency stimulation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed neurotechnology platform enables data streaming for tracking seizures and comorbidities related to memory, mood, and sleep. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights the potential of synchronized brain sensing and dense behavioral tracking during ANT-DBS for optimizing neuromodulation therapy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the platform as synchronized brain sensing and dense behavioral tracking with potential for therapy optimization, which clarifies and reframes the preprint's platform claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Artificial Intelligence algorithms provided accurate catalogs of seizures, interictal epileptiform spikes, and wake-sleep brain states. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claims do not explicitly state that AI algorithms provided accurate catalogs, so this specific accuracy claim appears omitted from the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Every model showed performance degradation in response to one or more tissue contaminants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Every model showed performance degradation in response to one or more tissue contaminants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same overarching result that models degrade with tissue contaminants.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DA detection balanced accuracy decreased from 0.74 to 0.69 +/- 0.01 with the addition of 1 patch of prostate tissue for every 100 patches of placenta. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Decidual arteriopathy detection balanced accuracy decreased from 0.74 to 0.69 ± 0.01 with the addition of 1 patch of prostate tissue for every 100 patches of placenta. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the exact quantitative degradation result for decidual arteriopathy detection.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Addition of bladder to prostate cancer needle biopsies induced false positives, resulting in a 97% false positive rate when added to needle biopsies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bladder, added at 10% contaminant, raised the mean absolute error in estimating gestational age from 1.626 weeks to 2.371 ± 0.003 weeks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims concern bladder contamination causing model errors, but the publication reports a different experiment/outcome (gestational age MAE) rather than the prostate-biopsy false positive rate, indicating a substantive revision of the specific result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tissue contaminants induce errors in modern ML models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tissue contaminants induce errors in modern ML models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same conclusion that tissue contaminants cause errors in machine learning models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The high level of attention given to contaminants indicates a failure to encode biological phenomena. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The high level of attention given to contaminants indicates a failure to encode biological phenomena. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the interpretive conclusion that model attention to contaminants reflects a failure to encode true biological signals.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining H&E-stained Whole Slide Images with proteomic signatures using a multimodal deep learning framework significantly improves the prediction of platinum response in both discovery and validation cohorts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining H&E-stained whole slide images with proteomic signatures using a multimodal deep learning framework significantly improves the prediction of platinum response in both discovery and validation cohorts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is effectively identical in scope and certainty to the preprint result, so no substantive change is detected.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This method outperforms the Homologous Recombination Deficiency (HRD) score in predicting platinum response and overall patient survival. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This method outperforms the Homologous Recombination Deficiency (HRD) score in predicting platinum response and overall patient survival. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this performance claim verbatim with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study sets new performance benchmarks and explores the intersection of histology and proteomics, highlighting phenotypes related to treatment response pathways. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Histology and proteomics contain complementary information about biological processes determining response to first line platinum treatment in HGSOC. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses the claim on complementary biological information determining response, dropping the explicit 'new performance benchmarks' and the broader phrasing about exploring phenotypes, so the claim's emphasis is revised and somewhat narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This integrative approach has the potential to improve personalized treatment and provide insights into the therapeutic vulnerabilities of HGSOC. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This integrative approach has the potential to improve personalized treatment and provide insights into the therapeutic vulnerabilities of HGSOC. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding translational potential is restated in the publication with the same content and level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RiskPath is an explainable AI toolbox that provides advanced timeseries methods for risk stratification in longitudinal cohorts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RiskPath is an explainable AI toolbox that provides advanced time-series methods for risk stratification in longitudinal cohorts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this core contribution with only minor stylistic normalization of 'time-series'.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The toolbox integrates theoretically-informed optimization to specify optimal model topology and explore performance-complexity tradeoffs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The toolbox integrates theoretically informed optimization to specify optimal model topology and explore performance-complexity trade-offs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this methodological contribution with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RiskPath includes modules that allow users to map the changing importance of predictors over the disease course. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RiskPath includes modules that allow users to visualize the changing importance of predictors over the disease course. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies 'map' to 'visualize', a more specific description of the module functionality without changing the claim's scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The toolbox enables visualization of the most important antecedent time epochs contributing to disease risk. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The specific phrasing about visualizing antecedent time epochs does not appear in the publication; instead the publication adds a claim about constructing compact models, indicating this particular preprint emphasis was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The supervised machine learning pipeline developed can distinguish between influenza and COVID-19 infections with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The supervised machine learning pipeline developed can distinguish between influenza and COVID-19 infections with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the high-accuracy discrimination result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (ROC AUC) of at least 97% using a multiclass classifier. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (ROC AUC) of at least 97% using a multiclass classifier. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the multiclass ROC AUC summary claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The influenza vs. COVID-19-positive model had an AUC of 98% on the internal test set and 92% on the external test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The influenza vs COVID-19-positive model had an AUC of 98.8% on the internal test set and 92.8% on the external test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides more precise AUC values (98.8% and 92.8%) compared to the rounded numbers in the preprint, refining but not changing the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study illustrates the potential of machine-learning models for accurately distinguishing between influenza and COVID-19. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study illustrates the potential of machine-learning models as a frontline diagnostic tool for distinguishing between influenza and COVID-19. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the potential application as a 'frontline diagnostic tool', narrowing the general conclusion to a particular clinical use while retaining similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The code developed may have utility as a frontline diagnostic tool for healthcare workers in triaging patients. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study illustrates the potential of machine-learning models as a frontline diagnostic tool for distinguishing between influenza and COVID-19. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes 'code' to 'models' but conveys the same practical potential as a frontline diagnostic application, so the claim is clarified but not substantially changed in scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A simple string pulling behavior task provides a reliable readout of shoulder health across animals and humans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A simple string pulling behavior task provides a reliable readout of shoulder mobility across animals and humans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines 'shoulder health' to the more specific 'shoulder mobility'; this clarifies but does not materially change the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mice and humans with rotator cuff tears exhibit decreased movement amplitude, prolonged movement time, and quantitative changes in waveform shape during string pulling task performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both mice and humans with rotator cuff tears exhibit decreased movement amplitude, prolonged movement time, and quantitative changes in waveform shape during the string pulling task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this experimental observation with essentially identical content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A predictive model built on a biomarker ensemble classifies human patients as having a rotator cuff tear with over 90% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A logistic regression model built on a biomarker ensemble classifies human patients as having a rotator cuff tear with over 90% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the model type (logistic regression), which clarifies the preprint's model description while preserving the accuracy claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combined framework of task kinematics, machine learning, and algorithmic assessment of movement quality enables the development of smartphone-based, at-home diagnostic tests for shoulder injury. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combined framework of animal models, motion capture, and algorithmic assessment of movement quality enables future research into smartphone-based diagnostic tests for shoulder injury. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication tones down the translational claim from enabling direct development of at-home diagnostics to enabling future research into such diagnostics, narrowing and tempering the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ML+R_t method outperforms the R_t Only method in identifying larger spikes in near-term SARS-CoV-2 incidence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ML+Rt method outperforms the Rt Only method in identifying larger spikes in SARS-CoV-2 incidence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this comparative performance claim with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both the ML+R_t and R_t Only methods performed well in predicting near-term SARS-CoV-2 increases, with correlation coefficients of 0.872 and 0.867 respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both the ML+Rt and Rt Only methods performed well in predicting near-term SARS-CoV-2 increases, with correlation coefficients of 0.872 and 0.867 respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the numeric performance comparison unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both methods perform adequately in predicting SARS-CoV-2 incidence in both rural and non-rural areas. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both methods perform adequately in predicting SARS-CoV-2 incidence in both rural and non-rural areas. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this geographic-performance claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study discusses practical issues regarding the implementation of forecasting models for public health action based on R_t. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study discusses practical issues regarding the implementation of forecasting models for public health action based on Rt. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same discussion-oriented conclusion about practical implementation issues.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A previously trained deep learning algorithm out-performed human experts in detecting congenital heart defects in a cohort where over 50 percent of cases were initially missed clinically. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A previously trained DL algorithm had higher sensitivity than initial clinical assessment in detecting CHD in a cohort in which over 50% of CHD cases were initially missed clinically. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm achieved a sensitivity of 91 percent and specificity of 93 percent. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A previously trained DL algorithm had higher sensitivity than initial clinical assessment in detecting CHD in a cohort in which over 50% of CHD cases were initially missed clinically. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the sensitivity-related performance claim but omits the specific sensitivity/specificity numbers from the preprint, so the numeric result was clarified/de-emphasized in the published text.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model performed well on community-acquired images in a low-risk population, including lesions it had not been previously exposed to. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DL algorithm performed well on community-acquired images in a low-risk population, including lesions to which it had not been exposed previously. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance observation with essentially identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When both the model and blinded human experts had access to stored images alone, the model outperformed expert humans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: When both the model and blinded human experts had access to only stored images, the model outperformed the human experts. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There was a statistically significant difference in model correctness by expert-grader quality score (p=0.04). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cardiorespiratory presentations are the most common subphenotype in pediatric Long COVID, present in 54% of patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cardiorespiratory presentations are the most common subphenotype in pediatric Long COVID, present in 54% of patients. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The exploratory analysis identified multiple subphenotypes of pediatric Long COVID, including musculoskeletal pain, neuropsychiatric conditions, gastrointestinal symptoms, headache, and fatigue. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: An unsupervised machine learning-based approach was successfully used to identify subphenotypes of pediatric Long COVID from electronic health record data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SWIFT predicts more than 80% of hypoxemic events in critically ill patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SWIFT predicts more than 80% of hypoxemic events in critically ill patients. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SWIFT predicts SpO <sub>2</sub> waveforms with average MSE below .0007. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The combined model integrating CT imaging features achieved 90% sensitivity, 64% specificity, and 95% negative predictive value at 25% prevalence. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified a lung-cancer specific transcriptional response to hypoxia in the assay readout. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The lung cancer-specific iCAP (LC-iCAP) demonstrated excellent reproducibility across various conditions and cell lineages. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individuals with Long COVID exhibit marked differences in specific circulating myeloid and lymphocyte populations compared to matched control groups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individuals with long COVID exhibit marked differences in circulating myeloid and lymphocyte populations compared to matched controls. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach could enable low-cost screening and proactive treatment for high-risk patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed model may have utility in clinical settings to guide the management and prognostication of COVID-19 patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Most trials did not explicitly exclude older adults with common chronic conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Known risk factors that may lead to severe illnesses have not been adequately considered in COVID-19 studies. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A careful examination of existing COVID-19 studies can inform future COVID-19 trial design towards balanced internal validity and generalizability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Risk factors such as diabetes, hypertension, and pregnancy were under-represented, likely skewing the population that was sampled. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Pregnant women were excluded by 34.9% of the studies. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three distinct sleep/circadian profiles were identified: active healthy sleepers, fragmented poor sleepers, and long and frequent nappers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Three distinct sleep/circadian profiles were identified: active healthy sleepers, fragmented poor sleepers, and long and frequent nappers. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Fragmented poor sleepers exhibit increased risks of dementia and cardiovascular disease events compared to active healthy sleepers. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Long and frequent nappers show a marginal association with increased cardiovascular disease events risk but not with dementia. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed model eCART demonstrated the highest AUROC of 0.835 in retrospective validation compared to NEWS and MEWS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed model eCARTv5 had the highest AUROC of 0.834 in retrospective validation compared to eCARTv2, NEWS, and MEWS. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: eCART's performance remained high with an AUROC of ≥0.80 across various patient demographics and clinical conditions during prospective validation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models trained on CLMBR often matched or were better than their ETE counterparts. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pretraining foundation models on electronic health records is a useful approach for developing clinical prediction models that perform well in the presence of temporal distribution shift. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed gamified web system aims to enhance the accuracy of diagnosing autism spectrum disorder and attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder through novel crowdsourcing algorithms and machine learning approaches. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The development of machine learning models that classify several conditions simultaneously can adaptively request additional information based on uncertainties about the data. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed AI-powered tool could identify complex social behaviors well enough to distinguish between conditions like ASD and ADHD. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

(certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PA of idiopathic PAH lungs showed lipid accumulation and altered expression of lipid homeostasis-related genes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide the largest publicly available MRI and iUS database of surgically treated brain tumors. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides the largest publicly available MRI and iUS database of surgically treated brain tumors. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This database is expected to help brain shift and image analysis research and neurosurgical training in interpreting iUS and iMRI. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The database is expected to help brain shift and image analysis research and neurosurgical training. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the expected benefits of the database for brain shift research, image analysis, and training with effectively the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Large language models (LLMs) are achieving near human-level performances on medical question and answering benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Conversational AI is likely to be developed for use in healthcare as LLMs achieve near human-level performances on medical question and answering benchmarks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Conversational AI is likely to be developed for use in healthcare as LLMs advance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Conversational AI is likely to be developed for use in healthcare as LLMs achieve near human-level performances on medical question and answering benchmarks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same prediction about conversational AI development tied to LLM performance, keeping the claim and certainty intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of ChatGPT and Foresight can be compared on the task of forecasting relevant diagnoses based on clinical vignettes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of ChatGPT and Foresight is compared on the task of forecasting relevant diagnoses based on clinical vignettes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries out and reports the same comparative evaluation between ChatGPT and Foresight.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are important considerations and limitations of transformer-based chatbots for clinical use. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are important considerations and limitations of transformer-based chatbots for clinical use. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same cautionary claim about limitations of transformer-based clinical chatbots.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep learning models achieved a cross-validated F1 score of 97.6% in scoring freezing of gait (FOG). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning models achieved a cross-validated F1 score of 97.6% for scoring FOG and 96.8% for identifying medication state. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models were able to identify medication state with a cross-validated F1 score of 96.8%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning models achieved a cross-validated F1 score of 97.6% for scoring FOG and 96.8% for identifying medication state. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the identical F1 (96.8%) for medication state identification, matching the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction error for total Parkinson's disease severity (MDS-UPDRS-III score) was ≤ 2.7 points. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific MDS-UPDRS-III prediction-error claim is not present among the publication claims and appears omitted from the summarized publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models can automatically and objectively score FOG with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates that deep learning models can automatically and objectively score freezing of gait (FOG) with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These models have the potential to discover novel kinematic biomarkers for FOG that can be used in clinical trials. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models have the potential to discover novel kinematic biomarkers for FOG that can be used for hypothesis generation and clinical trial outcome measures. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method uses the largest sample size to apply explainable, multi-task deep learning models for quantifying FOG. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We report a rapid and quantitative digital CRISPR-assisted HIV RNA detection assay implemented within a portable smartphone-based device. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We report a rapid and quantitative digital CRISPR-assisted HIV RNA detection assay implemented within a portable smartphone-based device. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim about a smartphone-based digital CRISPR assay with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed assay can detect HIV RNA at 42 °C in less than 30 minutes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed assay can efficiently detect HIV RNA at 42 °C. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our smartphone-enabled digital CRISPR device can detect 75 copies of HIV RNA in 15 minutes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using our smartphone-enabled digital CRISPR device, we successfully detected as low as 75 copies of HIV RNA in just 15 min. (certainty=high, type=result)
